value.Title: ZACHRY GROUP - COMPREHENSIVE INDUSTRY AND PEER NEWS UPDATES

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK8-2JG3-SBS5-0229-00000-00ZACHRY GROUP - COMPREHENSIVE INDUSTRY AND PEER NEWS UPDATES2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:09:33ZLexisNexisSTOCKZACHRY GROUP - COMPREHENSIVE INDUSTRY AND PEER NEWS UPDATES

DASHBOARD: ZACHRY GROUP

Websitehttps://www.zachrygroup.com/IndustryConstrustion

ACTIVITIES:

Zachry Group, founded in 1924, is a leading construction and engineering firm headquartered in San Antonio, Texas. Employing over 20,000 people, it provides turnkey services in power, energy, chemicals, and industrial sectors, generating over $3 billion annually. Its subsidiaries, like JVIC, offer maintenance and fabrication. In 2024, Zachry expanded renewable energy projects, including solar and wind facilities. Its culture emphasizes safety, citizenship, and employee training, with a commitment to community health and ethical practices. Competing with Fluor, Zachry stands out for its EPC (engineering, procurement, construction) expertise and safety standards. Recent initiatives include digital project management tools and carbon-neutral construction goals, aligning with sustainability trends.

+ ZACHRY GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ ZACHRY GROUP EXPANDS INTO OKLAHOMA WITH ROOSEVELT BRIDGE REPLACEMENT PROJECT

Zachry Construction Corporation has won a contract to replace the US-70 Roosevelt Bridge in Oklahoma, working in a joint venture with Traylor Bros., Inc. The Oklahoma Department of Transportation selected the Zachry-Traylor JV as the delivery partner, in what is the first collaboration between Zachry Construction and the OklahomaDOT.The announcement, dated 28th April 2026, underscores a significant upgrade to a corridor that connects Bryan and Marshall counties and serves travelers headed to Lake Texoma.The project will replace the aging structure with a new four-lane crossing featuring modern amenities including bicycle and pedestrian accommodations, a median barrier, and upgraded lighting. The existing bridge will be demolished, with traffic maintained on the current structure during construction to minimize disruption.Zachry Construction brings more than a century of experience in heavy and highway work, and the Traylor Bros. team is recognized for its expertise in bridge projects. Together they aim to deliver a state-of-the-art crossing that enhances connectivity and supports regional growth. Completion is targeted for 1st October 2029.

+ ZACHRY CONSTRUCTION PROMOTES MROSS TO PRESIDENT AS WATER AND INFRASTRUCTURE GROWTH ACCELERATES

Travis Mross has been elevated to president at Zachry Construction Corp., with the transition effective 29th April 2026. The promotion signals a continuing development plan for the San Antonio-based, family-owned contractor.Beginning as an intern in 2000 and rising to executive vice president for six years, Mross helped guide the firm's strategy and improve efficiency. He takes over from David Zachry, who will devote his efforts to CEO responsibilities.Zachry Construction remains a leader in heavy civil, roadway and infrastructure work, having completed more than 6,000 projects across public and private sectors. The company is expanding in water and wastewater and recently acquired Crescent Constructors Inc. to broaden its capabilities in that area.As president, Mross will oversee water, transportation, underground utilities and civil sitework, prioritizing growth, execution discipline and a strong corporate culture. He stated, "Our job is to bring consistency, discipline, and a high standard of execution to every project, which will guide how we serve clients and grow the business."

+ ZACHRY GROUP SECURES GROUND LEASE, ADDS PARKING LOTS TO SUPPORT MONARCH HOTEL

On Apr. 29, 2026, Zachry Group, through its affiliate Zachry Park Garage QOZB LLC, leased a parking lot from St. John's Evangelical Lutheran Church for a site across South Presa Street from the church near La Villita and Hemisfair, and acquired a smaller lot from St. Johns Four Square LLC, bringing the combined area to about 1.2 acres. Those parcels were part of a larger development plan by Dennis McDaniel in partnership with Opportunity Home San Antonio for 252 apartments and retail space, a plan that stalled during the pandemic.A Zachry representative said the spaces will support operational needs for the Monarch San Antonio hotel, which opened overlooking Civic Park. There are no immediate plans for additional development on the sites.Zachry Group, founded in 1924 by Henry Bartell 'Pat' Zachry, has a long-standing presence in San Antonio and was involved in the Monarch project, a roughly $185 million development that has helped shape the city’s hospitality landscape.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BECHTEL [3 News That Matters]

+ NABTU AND BECHTEL PARTNER TO DEVELOP THE NEXT-GENERATION NUCLEAR WORKFORCE

Bechtel and NABTU have signed an MOU to modernize apprenticeship programs for nuclear construction, as the United States pursues growth in nuclear energy including SMRs and large reactors, with the agreement signed on May 5, 2026. The agreement outlines steps to identify specialized craft skills, align curricula with industry requirements, and modernize training centers to reflect new construction methods. Leaders from Bechtel and NABTU stressed the importance of a robust training pipeline for the next generation of projects, citing Vogtle Units 3 and 4 and the Natrium Demonstration Project as benchmarks.

+ BECHTEL, EIMISA AGREE ON PARTNERSHIP TO ENHANCE REGIONAL MINING AND INFRASTRUCTURE DELIVERY

A global leader in engineering and construction has formed a strategic partnership with Echeverria Izquierdo Montajes Industriales S.A. (EIMISA), a prominent Chilean firm specializing in construction and industrial services. The announcement was made on 6th May 2026. The collaboration is designed to support the delivery of mining and infrastructure projects across Chile and South America.The partnership will leverage the strengths of both companies as mining investments surge, delivering projects that meet stringent safety and quality standards. The engineering and project management expertise of the global firm will complement EIMISA's direct-hire construction throughout Chile.Dario Barros Izquierdo, CEO of EIMISA, noted that the agreement builds on a foundation of trust between the two companies, adding, "Bechtel's global expertise and technical resources, combined with our local knowledge and execution capability, create a powerful team to support Chile's growing portfolio of complex projects."

+ SOWELA AND BECHTEL ROLL OUT FREE PIPEFITTING PROGRAM TO BOOST LOCAL WORKFORCE

Bechtel and SOWELA Technical Community College are partnering to offer a free Pipefitting Training Program at SOWELA's Main Campus in Lake Charles, aimed at preparing locals for careers in pipefitting across manufacturing, energy, and building systems. The program runs 26th May 2026 through 13th November 2026.The curriculum covers core job duties, safety principles, and the tools and materials used in the trade, with a strong emphasis on hands-on work installing and repairing high- and low-pressure piping systems.Classes run weekdays from 8 a.m. to 3 p.m. Upon completion, participants earn OSHA 10-hour credential and are positioned to pursue NCCER Pipefitting certifications, boosting their prospects in a crowded market.A background check and drug screening are required for employment consideration. Details for registration are at www.sowela.edu/pipefitting, and inquiries can be directed to SOWELA's Office of Workforce Solutions at (337) 421-6560 or workforce@sowela.edu

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KIEWIT CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ STRATEGIC JV SECURES MAJOR O &M CENTER FOR AUSTIN LIGHT RAIL AS PART OF $7.1B PROJECT

This announcement was made on 28th April 2026. A joint venture of Kiewit Building Group and Austin Commercial has moved forward with a design-build contract to construct an operations and maintenance facility for the US$7.1 billion Austin Light Rail line. The project will support a 10-mile corridor with 15 stations and all-electric trains planned to operate every five to ten minutes.The O&M facility will store, service, and dispatch trains and provide workspace for operators, maintenance crews, and support staff.Ten design-phase subcontractors are involved, and the overall effort is expected to generate thousands of jobs during both the design and construction phases. ATP Chief Executive Greg Canally noted the project as a major step forward and highlighted the value of a locally experienced partner like Kiewit.Earlier, Aecom-led LINC Austin was named the delivery partner for the first phase, with a roster that includes Parsons, STV, Turner & Townsend, Ardmore Roderick, and CAS Consulting.

+ KAP JV CHOSEN TO LEAD LIGHT RAIL O&M FACILITY AS PROJECT CONNECT ADVANCES

The Austin Transit Partnership revealed that Kiewit Austin Partnership (KAP), a Kiewit Building Group Inc. and Austin Commercial joint venture, will design and build the Operations and Maintenance Facility, accelerating pre-construction activities. The development was announced on 21st April 2026.The 62-acre OMF site will sit on the southwest edge of the Montopolis neighborhood, near U.S. 183 and SH 71, and will store and service up to 40 light rail vehicles. Property acquisitions tied to the project will affect dozens of existing businesses, with Airport Commerce Park highlighted as an area of relocation concern.KAP’s design team already includes about 10 subcontractors, with expectations for expanded involvement as construction ramps up. The broader Project Connect program is expected to generate substantial job activity across Texas during design and construction, while the OMF itself is projected to support around 300 long-term jobs once in service. Mainline construction could begin in 2027, with service anticipated for the early 2030s, following the Federal Transit Administration's January 2026 EIS/ROD clearance for final design and land acquisition.

+ STRATEGIC PARTNERSHIP ADVANCES U.S. CLEAN HYDROGEN AND HEAVY WATER PROJECT TO PRE-FEED

Aternium has selected Kiewit Engineering Group Inc. to conduct the pre-front-end engineering and design for its first integrated hydrogen and heavy water facility, aligning with the company's plan to deploy a $1 billion program to produce these materials domestically. The announcement was made on 26th March 2026.The Mid-Atlantic site will address demand for low-carbon energy and bolster U.S. capabilities in heavy water, which is currently sourced largely from foreign suppliers. Andrew Cottone noted that the collaboration with Kiewit reflects a shared commitment to technical excellence and safety.The project combines clean hydrogen production with a patent-pending heavy water isolation process, aiming to reduce hydrogen costs and strengthen domestic supply. The pre-FEED will set the scope and design foundations for future development, positioning the venture at the crossroads of energy transition and advanced manufacturing.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION ANNOUNCES 9,100-SF INNSBROOK EXPANSION UNDER COUNTY INCENTIVES

DPR Construction plans to expand its Innsbrook office by 9,100 square feet, backed by incentives from Henrico County. The date of this announcement is 29th April 2026.The expansion is designed to accommodate a growing workforce and enhance operations in the region.Officials say the project reflects confidence in the local market and will create additional job opportunities while reinforcing the firm’s sustainability commitments.The design will feature modern elements and sustainable practices, aligning with DPR’s mission to lead in sustainable construction.Completion is anticipated within the next year.

+ DPR TAPS JASON PAGE TO LEAD NASHVILLE SPECIAL SERVICES GROUP AND EXPAND INTERIOR BUILDOUTS

The SSG handles a range of work from parking garage stair installations to interior buildouts, and under Page's leadership the group aims to meet evolving client and stakeholder needs while Page mentors teams and promotes best practices for quick-turn, high-impact work.

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION ACHIEVES GREAT PLACE TO WORK CERTIFICATION WITH 97% POSITIVE EMPLOYEE FEEDBACK

DPR Construction has earned Great Place To Work Certification after 97 percent of its employees affirmed it is a great place to work, a rate about 40 points above the national average. The certification, awarded on 31st March 2026, highlights a culture built on pride, belonging and opportunity.According to CEO George Pfeffer, the certification reflects the real sentiments of the people who carry the company’s culture. "Being Certified(TradeMark) as a great place to work is especially meaningful because it comes directly from our people, who bring our culture to life," he said, underscoring an emphasis on trust, inclusion, and growth.DPR’s approach centers on empowering employees to make decisions and share information openly across projects. This openness supports initiative, whether in innovating on complex tasks, taking on new roles, or offering ideas outside formal job descriptions, with learning and development programs backing career progression.The recognition complements DPR’s broader reputation as an employer of choice and its ongoing achievements, including industry accolades and expansion across markets. DPR, founded in 1990, has grown into a multi billion dollar contractor employing over 11,000 professionals worldwide, with innovations such as virtual design and construction and self performing craft.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ PARSONS CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ PARSONS CORPORATION (NYSE:PSN) DEPLOYS SBIR-BACKED AUTONOMOUS DRONE INITIATIVE WITH EVERYWHERE COMMUNICATIONS

Parsons Corporation (NYSE:PSN) and EVERYWHERE Communications established a strategic partnership to push autonomous drone technology under SBIR, focusing on operations in disconnected environments. The partnership was confirmed on 4th of May 2026. By leveraging Iridium Satellite technology, EVERYWHERE Communications introduces a resilient data transport layer that enables drones to operate autonomously while transmitting sensor data back to operators and command systems. The platform supports beyond-line-of-sight operations, reliable data exfiltration and autonomous mission execution, with scalable coordination for multiple drones on low-bandwidth links. Jake Bailey, President of EVERYWHERE Communications, states, "In austere environments, connectivity is never guaranteed. We're enabling drones to keep operating—and keep delivering intelligence—even when the network is compromised." Parsons contributes TAK-as-a-Service (TaaS) to provide scalable, mission-ready integration and sustainment services tailored for the Department of War and federal agencies, enhancing real-time situational awareness and data interoperability. Mike Kushin, President of Defense and Intelligence at Parsons, notes, "In an era where every second and every signal matters, this collaboration brings together resilient autonomy in the air and trusted mission systems on the ground to give our warfighters and intelligence professionals a decisive information advantage." The parties aim to redefine operational capabilities for mission-critical teams worldwide.

+ PARSONS CORPORATION (NYSE: PSN) REPORTS COLLABORATION WITH THE BORING COMPANY ON DUBAI LOOP DESIGN VERIFICATION AND PERMITTING

CHANTILLY, Va. — The Boring Company awards Parsons a nine-month contract for the Dubai Loop project, dated 5th May 2026Parsons will act as TBC’s delegated program manager, delivering Independent Design Verification, Stakeholder Management, and Permitting and NOC Support for the pilot phase, and conducting multidisciplinary reviews across civil, structural, mechanical, electrical, safety, and utility disciplinesPierre Santoni, president of Infrastructure EMEA at Parsons, said, "Parsons has proudly played a central role in developing some of the most important and prestigious critical infrastructure programs across the UAE since our first programs there in the 1980s." The partnership with TBC aligns with the UAE leadership's Dubai 2040 Urban Master Plan and aims to advance local capabilities through collaborationThe Dubai Loop project, in collaboration with Dubai’s Roads and Transit Authority (RTA), seeks to expand urban mobility through tunnels and stations. Parsons has worked with the RTA since 2005, contributing to key projects such as the Dubai Metro and various highway and bridge programsParsons has managed more than 250 tunneling projects globally and has extensive road and highway experience covering over 10,000 miles on six continents

+ PARSONS CORPORATION (NYSE:PSN) EXPANDS NYC INFRASTRUCTURE ROLE WITH NEWTOWN CREEK TUNNEL CONTRACT AND USCG JV PROGRAM MANAGEMENT

Parsons Corporation (NYSE:PSN) Selected To Manage The Newtown Creek CSO Storage Tunnel For NYC DEP Following A Contract Award On Apr. 14, 2026. The Project Calls For A Large-Diameter Storage Tunnel And A Dewatering Pump Station To Protect Local WaterwaysThe New York City Department Of Environmental Protection Cites Parsons Experience On More Than 250 Tunnel And Ocean Outfall Projects Worldwide. The Initiative Aims To Modernize Municipal Assets And Improve Water Quality Through A Technology-Driven ApproachParsons Also Forms A Joint Venture With GSI Americas To Secure A US Coast Guard Program Management Contract Worth $25 Million. The Agreement Supports The USCG Execution Plan, Including Shore Infrastructure Upgrades And Deployment Of Transformational Technologies

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

value.Title: ZACHRY GROUP - UPDATES FROM BECHTEL CORPORATION AND PEERS: RECENT INSIGHTS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK1-XSF3-SHYX-40NH-00000-00ZACHRY GROUP - UPDATES FROM BECHTEL CORPORATION AND PEERS: RECENT INSIGHTS2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:09:33ZLexisNexisSTOCKZACHRY GROUP - UPDATES FROM BECHTEL CORPORATION AND PEERS: RECENT INSIGHTS

DASHBOARD: ZACHRY GROUP

Websitehttps://www.zachrygroup.com/IndustryConstrustion

ACTIVITIES:

Zachry Group, founded in 1924, is a leading construction and engineering firm headquartered in San Antonio, Texas. Employing over 20,000 people, it provides turnkey services in power, energy, chemicals, and industrial sectors, generating over $3 billion annually. Its subsidiaries, like JVIC, offer maintenance and fabrication. In 2024, Zachry expanded renewable energy projects, including solar and wind facilities. Its culture emphasizes safety, citizenship, and employee training, with a commitment to community health and ethical practices. Competing with Fluor, Zachry stands out for its EPC (engineering, procurement, construction) expertise and safety standards. Recent initiatives include digital project management tools and carbon-neutral construction goals, aligning with sustainability trends.

+ ZACHRY GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ ZACHRY GROUP EXPANDS INTO OKLAHOMA WITH ROOSEVELT BRIDGE REPLACEMENT PROJECT

Zachry Construction Corporation has won a contract to replace the US-70 Roosevelt Bridge in Oklahoma, working in a joint venture with Traylor Bros., Inc. The Oklahoma Department of Transportation selected the Zachry-Traylor JV as the delivery partner, in what is the first collaboration between Zachry Construction and the OklahomaDOT.The announcement, dated 28th April 2026, underscores a significant upgrade to a corridor that connects Bryan and Marshall counties and serves travelers headed to Lake Texoma.The project will replace the aging structure with a new four-lane crossing featuring modern amenities including bicycle and pedestrian accommodations, a median barrier, and upgraded lighting. The existing bridge will be demolished, with traffic maintained on the current structure during construction to minimize disruption.Zachry Construction brings more than a century of experience in heavy and highway work, and the Traylor Bros. team is recognized for its expertise in bridge projects. Together they aim to deliver a state-of-the-art crossing that enhances connectivity and supports regional growth. Completion is targeted for 1st October 2029.

+ ZACHRY CONSTRUCTION PROMOTES MROSS TO PRESIDENT AS WATER AND INFRASTRUCTURE GROWTH ACCELERATES

Travis Mross has been elevated to president at Zachry Construction Corp., with the transition effective 29th April 2026. The promotion signals a continuing development plan for the San Antonio-based, family-owned contractor.Beginning as an intern in 2000 and rising to executive vice president for six years, Mross helped guide the firm's strategy and improve efficiency. He takes over from David Zachry, who will devote his efforts to CEO responsibilities.Zachry Construction remains a leader in heavy civil, roadway and infrastructure work, having completed more than 6,000 projects across public and private sectors. The company is expanding in water and wastewater and recently acquired Crescent Constructors Inc. to broaden its capabilities in that area.As president, Mross will oversee water, transportation, underground utilities and civil sitework, prioritizing growth, execution discipline and a strong corporate culture. He stated, "Our job is to bring consistency, discipline, and a high standard of execution to every project, which will guide how we serve clients and grow the business."

+ ZACHRY GROUP SECURES GROUND LEASE, ADDS PARKING LOTS TO SUPPORT MONARCH HOTEL

On Apr. 29, 2026, Zachry Group, through its affiliate Zachry Park Garage QOZB LLC, leased a parking lot from St. John's Evangelical Lutheran Church for a site across South Presa Street from the church near La Villita and Hemisfair, and acquired a smaller lot from St. Johns Four Square LLC, bringing the combined area to about 1.2 acres. Those parcels were part of a larger development plan by Dennis McDaniel in partnership with Opportunity Home San Antonio for 252 apartments and retail space, a plan that stalled during the pandemic.A Zachry representative said the spaces will support operational needs for the Monarch San Antonio hotel, which opened overlooking Civic Park. There are no immediate plans for additional development on the sites.Zachry Group, founded in 1924 by Henry Bartell 'Pat' Zachry, has a long-standing presence in San Antonio and was involved in the Monarch project, a roughly $185 million development that has helped shape the city’s hospitality landscape.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BECHTEL [3 News That Matters]

+ NABTU AND BECHTEL PARTNER TO DEVELOP THE NEXT-GENERATION NUCLEAR WORKFORCE

Bechtel and NABTU have signed an MOU to modernize apprenticeship programs for nuclear construction, as the United States pursues growth in nuclear energy including SMRs and large reactors, with the agreement signed on May 5, 2026. The agreement outlines steps to identify specialized craft skills, align curricula with industry requirements, and modernize training centers to reflect new construction methods. Leaders from Bechtel and NABTU stressed the importance of a robust training pipeline for the next generation of projects, citing Vogtle Units 3 and 4 and the Natrium Demonstration Project as benchmarks.

+ BECHTEL, EIMISA AGREE ON PARTNERSHIP TO ENHANCE REGIONAL MINING AND INFRASTRUCTURE DELIVERY

A global leader in engineering and construction has formed a strategic partnership with Echeverria Izquierdo Montajes Industriales S.A. (EIMISA), a prominent Chilean firm specializing in construction and industrial services. The announcement was made on 6th May 2026. The collaboration is designed to support the delivery of mining and infrastructure projects across Chile and South America.The partnership will leverage the strengths of both companies as mining investments surge, delivering projects that meet stringent safety and quality standards. The engineering and project management expertise of the global firm will complement EIMISA's direct-hire construction throughout Chile.Dario Barros Izquierdo, CEO of EIMISA, noted that the agreement builds on a foundation of trust between the two companies, adding, "Bechtel's global expertise and technical resources, combined with our local knowledge and execution capability, create a powerful team to support Chile's growing portfolio of complex projects."

+ SOWELA AND BECHTEL ROLL OUT FREE PIPEFITTING PROGRAM TO BOOST LOCAL WORKFORCE

Bechtel and SOWELA Technical Community College are partnering to offer a free Pipefitting Training Program at SOWELA's Main Campus in Lake Charles, aimed at preparing locals for careers in pipefitting across manufacturing, energy, and building systems. The program runs 26th May 2026 through 13th November 2026.The curriculum covers core job duties, safety principles, and the tools and materials used in the trade, with a strong emphasis on hands-on work installing and repairing high- and low-pressure piping systems.Classes run weekdays from 8 a.m. to 3 p.m. Upon completion, participants earn OSHA 10-hour credential and are positioned to pursue NCCER Pipefitting certifications, boosting their prospects in a crowded market.A background check and drug screening are required for employment consideration. Details for registration are at www.sowela.edu/pipefitting, and inquiries can be directed to SOWELA's Office of Workforce Solutions at (337) 421-6560 or workforce@sowela.edu

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KIEWIT CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ STRATEGIC JV SECURES MAJOR O &M CENTER FOR AUSTIN LIGHT RAIL AS PART OF $7.1B PROJECT

This announcement was made on 28th April 2026. A joint venture of Kiewit Building Group and Austin Commercial has moved forward with a design-build contract to construct an operations and maintenance facility for the US$7.1 billion Austin Light Rail line. The project will support a 10-mile corridor with 15 stations and all-electric trains planned to operate every five to ten minutes.The O&M facility will store, service, and dispatch trains and provide workspace for operators, maintenance crews, and support staff.Ten design-phase subcontractors are involved, and the overall effort is expected to generate thousands of jobs during both the design and construction phases. ATP Chief Executive Greg Canally noted the project as a major step forward and highlighted the value of a locally experienced partner like Kiewit.Earlier, Aecom-led LINC Austin was named the delivery partner for the first phase, with a roster that includes Parsons, STV, Turner & Townsend, Ardmore Roderick, and CAS Consulting.

+ KAP JV CHOSEN TO LEAD LIGHT RAIL O&M FACILITY AS PROJECT CONNECT ADVANCES

The Austin Transit Partnership revealed that Kiewit Austin Partnership (KAP), a Kiewit Building Group Inc. and Austin Commercial joint venture, will design and build the Operations and Maintenance Facility, accelerating pre-construction activities. The development was announced on 21st April 2026.The 62-acre OMF site will sit on the southwest edge of the Montopolis neighborhood, near U.S. 183 and SH 71, and will store and service up to 40 light rail vehicles. Property acquisitions tied to the project will affect dozens of existing businesses, with Airport Commerce Park highlighted as an area of relocation concern.KAP’s design team already includes about 10 subcontractors, with expectations for expanded involvement as construction ramps up. The broader Project Connect program is expected to generate substantial job activity across Texas during design and construction, while the OMF itself is projected to support around 300 long-term jobs once in service. Mainline construction could begin in 2027, with service anticipated for the early 2030s, following the Federal Transit Administration's January 2026 EIS/ROD clearance for final design and land acquisition.

+ STRATEGIC PARTNERSHIP ADVANCES U.S. CLEAN HYDROGEN AND HEAVY WATER PROJECT TO PRE-FEED

Aternium has selected Kiewit Engineering Group Inc. to conduct the pre-front-end engineering and design for its first integrated hydrogen and heavy water facility, aligning with the company's plan to deploy a $1 billion program to produce these materials domestically. The announcement was made on 26th March 2026.The Mid-Atlantic site will address demand for low-carbon energy and bolster U.S. capabilities in heavy water, which is currently sourced largely from foreign suppliers. Andrew Cottone noted that the collaboration with Kiewit reflects a shared commitment to technical excellence and safety.The project combines clean hydrogen production with a patent-pending heavy water isolation process, aiming to reduce hydrogen costs and strengthen domestic supply. The pre-FEED will set the scope and design foundations for future development, positioning the venture at the crossroads of energy transition and advanced manufacturing.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION ANNOUNCES 9,100-SF INNSBROOK EXPANSION UNDER COUNTY INCENTIVES

DPR Construction plans to expand its Innsbrook office by 9,100 square feet, backed by incentives from Henrico County. The date of this announcement is 29th April 2026.The expansion is designed to accommodate a growing workforce and enhance operations in the region.Officials say the project reflects confidence in the local market and will create additional job opportunities while reinforcing the firm’s sustainability commitments.The design will feature modern elements and sustainable practices, aligning with DPR’s mission to lead in sustainable construction.Completion is anticipated within the next year.

+ DPR TAPS JASON PAGE TO LEAD NASHVILLE SPECIAL SERVICES GROUP AND EXPAND INTERIOR BUILDOUTS

The SSG handles a range of work from parking garage stair installations to interior buildouts, and under Page's leadership the group aims to meet evolving client and stakeholder needs while Page mentors teams and promotes best practices for quick-turn, high-impact work.

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION ACHIEVES GREAT PLACE TO WORK CERTIFICATION WITH 97% POSITIVE EMPLOYEE FEEDBACK

DPR Construction has earned Great Place To Work Certification after 97 percent of its employees affirmed it is a great place to work, a rate about 40 points above the national average. The certification, awarded on 31st March 2026, highlights a culture built on pride, belonging and opportunity.According to CEO George Pfeffer, the certification reflects the real sentiments of the people who carry the company’s culture. "Being Certified(TradeMark) as a great place to work is especially meaningful because it comes directly from our people, who bring our culture to life," he said, underscoring an emphasis on trust, inclusion, and growth.DPR’s approach centers on empowering employees to make decisions and share information openly across projects. This openness supports initiative, whether in innovating on complex tasks, taking on new roles, or offering ideas outside formal job descriptions, with learning and development programs backing career progression.The recognition complements DPR’s broader reputation as an employer of choice and its ongoing achievements, including industry accolades and expansion across markets. DPR, founded in 1990, has grown into a multi billion dollar contractor employing over 11,000 professionals worldwide, with innovations such as virtual design and construction and self performing craft.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ PARSONS CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ PARSONS CORPORATION (NYSE:PSN) DEPLOYS SBIR-BACKED AUTONOMOUS DRONE INITIATIVE WITH EVERYWHERE COMMUNICATIONS

Parsons Corporation (NYSE:PSN) and EVERYWHERE Communications established a strategic partnership to push autonomous drone technology under SBIR, focusing on operations in disconnected environments. The partnership was confirmed on 4th of May 2026. By leveraging Iridium Satellite technology, EVERYWHERE Communications introduces a resilient data transport layer that enables drones to operate autonomously while transmitting sensor data back to operators and command systems. The platform supports beyond-line-of-sight operations, reliable data exfiltration and autonomous mission execution, with scalable coordination for multiple drones on low-bandwidth links. Jake Bailey, President of EVERYWHERE Communications, states, "In austere environments, connectivity is never guaranteed. We're enabling drones to keep operating—and keep delivering intelligence—even when the network is compromised." Parsons contributes TAK-as-a-Service (TaaS) to provide scalable, mission-ready integration and sustainment services tailored for the Department of War and federal agencies, enhancing real-time situational awareness and data interoperability. Mike Kushin, President of Defense and Intelligence at Parsons, notes, "In an era where every second and every signal matters, this collaboration brings together resilient autonomy in the air and trusted mission systems on the ground to give our warfighters and intelligence professionals a decisive information advantage." The parties aim to redefine operational capabilities for mission-critical teams worldwide.

+ PARSONS CORPORATION (NYSE: PSN) REPORTS COLLABORATION WITH THE BORING COMPANY ON DUBAI LOOP DESIGN VERIFICATION AND PERMITTING

CHANTILLY, Va. — The Boring Company awards Parsons a nine-month contract for the Dubai Loop project, dated 5th May 2026Parsons will act as TBC’s delegated program manager, delivering Independent Design Verification, Stakeholder Management, and Permitting and NOC Support for the pilot phase, and conducting multidisciplinary reviews across civil, structural, mechanical, electrical, safety, and utility disciplinesPierre Santoni, president of Infrastructure EMEA at Parsons, said, "Parsons has proudly played a central role in developing some of the most important and prestigious critical infrastructure programs across the UAE since our first programs there in the 1980s." The partnership with TBC aligns with the UAE leadership's Dubai 2040 Urban Master Plan and aims to advance local capabilities through collaborationThe Dubai Loop project, in collaboration with Dubai’s Roads and Transit Authority (RTA), seeks to expand urban mobility through tunnels and stations. Parsons has worked with the RTA since 2005, contributing to key projects such as the Dubai Metro and various highway and bridge programsParsons has managed more than 250 tunneling projects globally and has extensive road and highway experience covering over 10,000 miles on six continents

+ PARSONS CORPORATION (NYSE:PSN) EXPANDS NYC INFRASTRUCTURE ROLE WITH NEWTOWN CREEK TUNNEL CONTRACT AND USCG JV PROGRAM MANAGEMENT

Parsons Corporation (NYSE:PSN) Selected To Manage The Newtown Creek CSO Storage Tunnel For NYC DEP Following A Contract Award On Apr. 14, 2026. The Project Calls For A Large-Diameter Storage Tunnel And A Dewatering Pump Station To Protect Local WaterwaysThe New York City Department Of Environmental Protection Cites Parsons Experience On More Than 250 Tunnel And Ocean Outfall Projects Worldwide. The Initiative Aims To Modernize Municipal Assets And Improve Water Quality Through A Technology-Driven ApproachParsons Also Forms A Joint Venture With GSI Americas To Secure A US Coast Guard Program Management Contract Worth $25 Million. The Agreement Supports The USCG Execution Plan, Including Shore Infrastructure Upgrades And Deployment Of Transformational Technologies

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

urn:contentItem:6JJT-YG93-S384-J3TC-00000-00ZACHRY GROUP - BECHTEL CORPORATION AND PEERS: RECENT INDUSTRY UPDATES2026-05-12T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:09:33ZLexisNexisSTOCKZACHRY GROUP - BECHTEL CORPORATION AND PEERS: RECENT INDUSTRY UPDATES

DASHBOARD: ZACHRY GROUP

Websitehttps://www.zachrygroup.com/IndustryConstrustion

ACTIVITIES:

Zachry Group, founded in 1924, is a leading construction and engineering firm headquartered in San Antonio, Texas. Employing over 20,000 people, it provides turnkey services in power, energy, chemicals, and industrial sectors, generating over $3 billion annually. Its subsidiaries, like JVIC, offer maintenance and fabrication. In 2024, Zachry expanded renewable energy projects, including solar and wind facilities. Its culture emphasizes safety, citizenship, and employee training, with a commitment to community health and ethical practices. Competing with Fluor, Zachry stands out for its EPC (engineering, procurement, construction) expertise and safety standards. Recent initiatives include digital project management tools and carbon-neutral construction goals, aligning with sustainability trends.

+ ZACHRY GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ ZACHRY GROUP EXPANDS INTO OKLAHOMA WITH ROOSEVELT BRIDGE REPLACEMENT PROJECT

Zachry Construction Corporation has won a contract to replace the US-70 Roosevelt Bridge in Oklahoma, working in a joint venture with Traylor Bros., Inc. The Oklahoma Department of Transportation selected the Zachry-Traylor JV as the delivery partner, in what is the first collaboration between Zachry Construction and the OklahomaDOT.The announcement, dated 28th April 2026, underscores a significant upgrade to a corridor that connects Bryan and Marshall counties and serves travelers headed to Lake Texoma.The project will replace the aging structure with a new four-lane crossing featuring modern amenities including bicycle and pedestrian accommodations, a median barrier, and upgraded lighting. The existing bridge will be demolished, with traffic maintained on the current structure during construction to minimize disruption.Zachry Construction brings more than a century of experience in heavy and highway work, and the Traylor Bros. team is recognized for its expertise in bridge projects. Together they aim to deliver a state-of-the-art crossing that enhances connectivity and supports regional growth. Completion is targeted for 1st October 2029.

+ ZACHRY CONSTRUCTION PROMOTES MROSS TO PRESIDENT AS WATER AND INFRASTRUCTURE GROWTH ACCELERATES

Travis Mross has been elevated to president at Zachry Construction Corp., with the transition effective 29th April 2026. The promotion signals a continuing development plan for the San Antonio-based, family-owned contractor.Beginning as an intern in 2000 and rising to executive vice president for six years, Mross helped guide the firm's strategy and improve efficiency. He takes over from David Zachry, who will devote his efforts to CEO responsibilities.Zachry Construction remains a leader in heavy civil, roadway and infrastructure work, having completed more than 6,000 projects across public and private sectors. The company is expanding in water and wastewater and recently acquired Crescent Constructors Inc. to broaden its capabilities in that area.As president, Mross will oversee water, transportation, underground utilities and civil sitework, prioritizing growth, execution discipline and a strong corporate culture. He stated, "Our job is to bring consistency, discipline, and a high standard of execution to every project, which will guide how we serve clients and grow the business."

+ ZACHRY GROUP SECURES GROUND LEASE, ADDS PARKING LOTS TO SUPPORT MONARCH HOTEL

On Apr. 29, 2026, Zachry Group, through its affiliate Zachry Park Garage QOZB LLC, leased a parking lot from St. John's Evangelical Lutheran Church for a site across South Presa Street from the church near La Villita and Hemisfair, and acquired a smaller lot from St. Johns Four Square LLC, bringing the combined area to about 1.2 acres. Those parcels were part of a larger development plan by Dennis McDaniel in partnership with Opportunity Home San Antonio for 252 apartments and retail space, a plan that stalled during the pandemic.A Zachry representative said the spaces will support operational needs for the Monarch San Antonio hotel, which opened overlooking Civic Park. There are no immediate plans for additional development on the sites.Zachry Group, founded in 1924 by Henry Bartell 'Pat' Zachry, has a long-standing presence in San Antonio and was involved in the Monarch project, a roughly $185 million development that has helped shape the city’s hospitality landscape.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BECHTEL [3 News That Matters]

+ BECHTEL, EIMISA AGREE ON PARTNERSHIP TO ENHANCE REGIONAL MINING AND INFRASTRUCTURE DELIVERY

A global leader in engineering and construction has formed a strategic partnership with Echeverria Izquierdo Montajes Industriales S.A. (EIMISA), a prominent Chilean firm specializing in construction and industrial services. The announcement was made on 6th May 2026. The collaboration is designed to support the delivery of mining and infrastructure projects across Chile and South America.The partnership will leverage the strengths of both companies as mining investments surge, delivering projects that meet stringent safety and quality standards. The engineering and project management expertise of the global firm will complement EIMISA's direct-hire construction throughout Chile.Dario Barros Izquierdo, CEO of EIMISA, noted that the agreement builds on a foundation of trust between the two companies, adding, "Bechtel's global expertise and technical resources, combined with our local knowledge and execution capability, create a powerful team to support Chile's growing portfolio of complex projects."

+ NABTU AND BECHTEL PARTNER TO DEVELOP THE NEXT-GENERATION NUCLEAR WORKFORCE

Bechtel and NABTU have signed an MOU to modernize apprenticeship programs for nuclear construction, as the United States pursues growth in nuclear energy including SMRs and large reactors, with the agreement signed on May 5, 2026. The agreement outlines steps to identify specialized craft skills, align curricula with industry requirements, and modernize training centers to reflect new construction methods. Leaders from Bechtel and NABTU stressed the importance of a robust training pipeline for the next generation of projects, citing Vogtle Units 3 and 4 and the Natrium Demonstration Project as benchmarks.

+ SOWELA AND BECHTEL ROLL OUT FREE PIPEFITTING PROGRAM TO BOOST LOCAL WORKFORCE

Bechtel and SOWELA Technical Community College are partnering to offer a free Pipefitting Training Program at SOWELA's Main Campus in Lake Charles, aimed at preparing locals for careers in pipefitting across manufacturing, energy, and building systems. The program runs 26th May 2026 through 13th November 2026.The curriculum covers core job duties, safety principles, and the tools and materials used in the trade, with a strong emphasis on hands-on work installing and repairing high- and low-pressure piping systems.Classes run weekdays from 8 a.m. to 3 p.m. Upon completion, participants earn OSHA 10-hour credential and are positioned to pursue NCCER Pipefitting certifications, boosting their prospects in a crowded market.A background check and drug screening are required for employment consideration. Details for registration are at www.sowela.edu/pipefitting, and inquiries can be directed to SOWELA's Office of Workforce Solutions at (337) 421-6560 or workforce@sowela.edu

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KIEWIT CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ STRATEGIC JV SECURES MAJOR O &M CENTER FOR AUSTIN LIGHT RAIL AS PART OF $7.1B PROJECT

This announcement was made on 28th April 2026. A joint venture of Kiewit Building Group and Austin Commercial has moved forward with a design-build contract to construct an operations and maintenance facility for the US$7.1 billion Austin Light Rail line. The project will support a 10-mile corridor with 15 stations and all-electric trains planned to operate every five to ten minutes.The O&M facility will store, service, and dispatch trains and provide workspace for operators, maintenance crews, and support staff.Ten design-phase subcontractors are involved, and the overall effort is expected to generate thousands of jobs during both the design and construction phases. ATP Chief Executive Greg Canally noted the project as a major step forward and highlighted the value of a locally experienced partner like Kiewit.Earlier, Aecom-led LINC Austin was named the delivery partner for the first phase, with a roster that includes Parsons, STV, Turner & Townsend, Ardmore Roderick, and CAS Consulting.

+ KAP JV CHOSEN TO LEAD LIGHT RAIL O&M FACILITY AS PROJECT CONNECT ADVANCES

The Austin Transit Partnership revealed that Kiewit Austin Partnership (KAP), a Kiewit Building Group Inc. and Austin Commercial joint venture, will design and build the Operations and Maintenance Facility, accelerating pre-construction activities. The development was announced on 21st April 2026.The 62-acre OMF site will sit on the southwest edge of the Montopolis neighborhood, near U.S. 183 and SH 71, and will store and service up to 40 light rail vehicles. Property acquisitions tied to the project will affect dozens of existing businesses, with Airport Commerce Park highlighted as an area of relocation concern.KAP’s design team already includes about 10 subcontractors, with expectations for expanded involvement as construction ramps up. The broader Project Connect program is expected to generate substantial job activity across Texas during design and construction, while the OMF itself is projected to support around 300 long-term jobs once in service. Mainline construction could begin in 2027, with service anticipated for the early 2030s, following the Federal Transit Administration's January 2026 EIS/ROD clearance for final design and land acquisition.

+ STRATEGIC PARTNERSHIP ADVANCES U.S. CLEAN HYDROGEN AND HEAVY WATER PROJECT TO PRE-FEED

Aternium has selected Kiewit Engineering Group Inc. to conduct the pre-front-end engineering and design for its first integrated hydrogen and heavy water facility, aligning with the company's plan to deploy a $1 billion program to produce these materials domestically. The announcement was made on 26th March 2026.The Mid-Atlantic site will address demand for low-carbon energy and bolster U.S. capabilities in heavy water, which is currently sourced largely from foreign suppliers. Andrew Cottone noted that the collaboration with Kiewit reflects a shared commitment to technical excellence and safety.The project combines clean hydrogen production with a patent-pending heavy water isolation process, aiming to reduce hydrogen costs and strengthen domestic supply. The pre-FEED will set the scope and design foundations for future development, positioning the venture at the crossroads of energy transition and advanced manufacturing.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION ANNOUNCES 9,100-SF INNSBROOK EXPANSION UNDER COUNTY INCENTIVES

DPR Construction plans to expand its Innsbrook office by 9,100 square feet, backed by incentives from Henrico County. The date of this announcement is 29th April 2026.The expansion is designed to accommodate a growing workforce and enhance operations in the region.Officials say the project reflects confidence in the local market and will create additional job opportunities while reinforcing the firm’s sustainability commitments.The design will feature modern elements and sustainable practices, aligning with DPR’s mission to lead in sustainable construction.Completion is anticipated within the next year.

+ DPR TAPS JASON PAGE TO LEAD NASHVILLE SPECIAL SERVICES GROUP AND EXPAND INTERIOR BUILDOUTS

The SSG handles a range of work from parking garage stair installations to interior buildouts, and under Page's leadership the group aims to meet evolving client and stakeholder needs while Page mentors teams and promotes best practices for quick-turn, high-impact work.

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION ACHIEVES GREAT PLACE TO WORK CERTIFICATION WITH 97% POSITIVE EMPLOYEE FEEDBACK

DPR Construction has earned Great Place To Work Certification after 97 percent of its employees affirmed it is a great place to work, a rate about 40 points above the national average. The certification, awarded on 31st March 2026, highlights a culture built on pride, belonging and opportunity.According to CEO George Pfeffer, the certification reflects the real sentiments of the people who carry the company’s culture. "Being Certified(TradeMark) as a great place to work is especially meaningful because it comes directly from our people, who bring our culture to life," he said, underscoring an emphasis on trust, inclusion, and growth.DPR’s approach centers on empowering employees to make decisions and share information openly across projects. This openness supports initiative, whether in innovating on complex tasks, taking on new roles, or offering ideas outside formal job descriptions, with learning and development programs backing career progression.The recognition complements DPR’s broader reputation as an employer of choice and its ongoing achievements, including industry accolades and expansion across markets. DPR, founded in 1990, has grown into a multi billion dollar contractor employing over 11,000 professionals worldwide, with innovations such as virtual design and construction and self performing craft.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ PARSONS CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ PARSONS CORPORATION (NYSE:PSN) DEPLOYS SBIR-BACKED AUTONOMOUS DRONE INITIATIVE WITH EVERYWHERE COMMUNICATIONS

Parsons Corporation (NYSE:PSN) and EVERYWHERE Communications established a strategic partnership to push autonomous drone technology under SBIR, focusing on operations in disconnected environments. The partnership was confirmed on 4th of May 2026. By leveraging Iridium Satellite technology, EVERYWHERE Communications introduces a resilient data transport layer that enables drones to operate autonomously while transmitting sensor data back to operators and command systems. The platform supports beyond-line-of-sight operations, reliable data exfiltration and autonomous mission execution, with scalable coordination for multiple drones on low-bandwidth links. Jake Bailey, President of EVERYWHERE Communications, states, "In austere environments, connectivity is never guaranteed. We're enabling drones to keep operating—and keep delivering intelligence—even when the network is compromised." Parsons contributes TAK-as-a-Service (TaaS) to provide scalable, mission-ready integration and sustainment services tailored for the Department of War and federal agencies, enhancing real-time situational awareness and data interoperability. Mike Kushin, President of Defense and Intelligence at Parsons, notes, "In an era where every second and every signal matters, this collaboration brings together resilient autonomy in the air and trusted mission systems on the ground to give our warfighters and intelligence professionals a decisive information advantage." The parties aim to redefine operational capabilities for mission-critical teams worldwide.

+ PARSONS CORPORATION (NYSE: PSN) REPORTS COLLABORATION WITH THE BORING COMPANY ON DUBAI LOOP DESIGN VERIFICATION AND PERMITTING

CHANTILLY, Va. — The Boring Company awards Parsons a nine-month contract for the Dubai Loop project, dated 5th May 2026Parsons will act as TBC’s delegated program manager, delivering Independent Design Verification, Stakeholder Management, and Permitting and NOC Support for the pilot phase, and conducting multidisciplinary reviews across civil, structural, mechanical, electrical, safety, and utility disciplinesPierre Santoni, president of Infrastructure EMEA at Parsons, said, "Parsons has proudly played a central role in developing some of the most important and prestigious critical infrastructure programs across the UAE since our first programs there in the 1980s." The partnership with TBC aligns with the UAE leadership's Dubai 2040 Urban Master Plan and aims to advance local capabilities through collaborationThe Dubai Loop project, in collaboration with Dubai’s Roads and Transit Authority (RTA), seeks to expand urban mobility through tunnels and stations. Parsons has worked with the RTA since 2005, contributing to key projects such as the Dubai Metro and various highway and bridge programsParsons has managed more than 250 tunneling projects globally and has extensive road and highway experience covering over 10,000 miles on six continents

+ PARSONS CORPORATION (NYSE:PSN) EXPANDS NYC INFRASTRUCTURE ROLE WITH NEWTOWN CREEK TUNNEL CONTRACT AND USCG JV PROGRAM MANAGEMENT

Parsons Corporation (NYSE:PSN) Selected To Manage The Newtown Creek CSO Storage Tunnel For NYC DEP Following A Contract Award On Apr. 14, 2026. The Project Calls For A Large-Diameter Storage Tunnel And A Dewatering Pump Station To Protect Local WaterwaysThe New York City Department Of Environmental Protection Cites Parsons Experience On More Than 250 Tunnel And Ocean Outfall Projects Worldwide. The Initiative Aims To Modernize Municipal Assets And Improve Water Quality Through A Technology-Driven ApproachParsons Also Forms A Joint Venture With GSI Americas To Secure A US Coast Guard Program Management Contract Worth $25 Million. The Agreement Supports The USCG Execution Plan, Including Shore Infrastructure Upgrades And Deployment Of Transformational Technologies

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

value.Title: ZACHRY GROUP - RECENT NEWS OF PEER AND INDUSTRY INSIGHTS

urn:contentItem:6JJK-TSC3-RS8T-N00P-00000-00ZACHRY GROUP - RECENT NEWS OF PEER AND INDUSTRY INSIGHTS2026-05-11T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:09:33ZLexisNexisSTOCKZACHRY GROUP - RECENT NEWS OF PEER AND INDUSTRY INSIGHTS

DASHBOARD: ZACHRY GROUP

Websitehttps://www.zachrygroup.com/IndustryConstrustion

ACTIVITIES:

Zachry Group, founded in 1924, is a leading construction and engineering firm headquartered in San Antonio, Texas. Employing over 20,000 people, it provides turnkey services in power, energy, chemicals, and industrial sectors, generating over $3 billion annually. Its subsidiaries, like JVIC, offer maintenance and fabrication. In 2024, Zachry expanded renewable energy projects, including solar and wind facilities. Its culture emphasizes safety, citizenship, and employee training, with a commitment to community health and ethical practices. Competing with Fluor, Zachry stands out for its EPC (engineering, procurement, construction) expertise and safety standards. Recent initiatives include digital project management tools and carbon-neutral construction goals, aligning with sustainability trends.

+ ZACHRY GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ ZACHRY GROUP EXPANDS INTO OKLAHOMA WITH ROOSEVELT BRIDGE REPLACEMENT PROJECT

Zachry Construction Corporation has won a contract to replace the US-70 Roosevelt Bridge in Oklahoma, working in a joint venture with Traylor Bros., Inc. The Oklahoma Department of Transportation selected the Zachry-Traylor JV as the delivery partner, in what is the first collaboration between Zachry Construction and the OklahomaDOT.The announcement, dated 28th April 2026, underscores a significant upgrade to a corridor that connects Bryan and Marshall counties and serves travelers headed to Lake Texoma.The project will replace the aging structure with a new four-lane crossing featuring modern amenities including bicycle and pedestrian accommodations, a median barrier, and upgraded lighting. The existing bridge will be demolished, with traffic maintained on the current structure during construction to minimize disruption.Zachry Construction brings more than a century of experience in heavy and highway work, and the Traylor Bros. team is recognized for its expertise in bridge projects. Together they aim to deliver a state-of-the-art crossing that enhances connectivity and supports regional growth. Completion is targeted for 1st October 2029.

+ ZACHRY CONSTRUCTION PROMOTES MROSS TO PRESIDENT AS WATER AND INFRASTRUCTURE GROWTH ACCELERATES

Travis Mross has been elevated to president at Zachry Construction Corp., with the transition effective 29th April 2026. The promotion signals a continuing development plan for the San Antonio-based, family-owned contractor.Beginning as an intern in 2000 and rising to executive vice president for six years, Mross helped guide the firm's strategy and improve efficiency. He takes over from David Zachry, who will devote his efforts to CEO responsibilities.Zachry Construction remains a leader in heavy civil, roadway and infrastructure work, having completed more than 6,000 projects across public and private sectors. The company is expanding in water and wastewater and recently acquired Crescent Constructors Inc. to broaden its capabilities in that area.As president, Mross will oversee water, transportation, underground utilities and civil sitework, prioritizing growth, execution discipline and a strong corporate culture. He stated, "Our job is to bring consistency, discipline, and a high standard of execution to every project, which will guide how we serve clients and grow the business."

+ ZACHRY GROUP SECURES GROUND LEASE, ADDS PARKING LOTS TO SUPPORT MONARCH HOTEL

On Apr. 29, 2026, Zachry Group, through its affiliate Zachry Park Garage QOZB LLC, leased a parking lot from St. John's Evangelical Lutheran Church for a site across South Presa Street from the church near La Villita and Hemisfair, and acquired a smaller lot from St. Johns Four Square LLC, bringing the combined area to about 1.2 acres. Those parcels were part of a larger development plan by Dennis McDaniel in partnership with Opportunity Home San Antonio for 252 apartments and retail space, a plan that stalled during the pandemic.A Zachry representative said the spaces will support operational needs for the Monarch San Antonio hotel, which opened overlooking Civic Park. There are no immediate plans for additional development on the sites.Zachry Group, founded in 1924 by Henry Bartell 'Pat' Zachry, has a long-standing presence in San Antonio and was involved in the Monarch project, a roughly $185 million development that has helped shape the city’s hospitality landscape.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BECHTEL [3 News That Matters]

+ BECHTEL, EIMISA AGREE ON PARTNERSHIP TO ENHANCE REGIONAL MINING AND INFRASTRUCTURE DELIVERY

A global leader in engineering and construction has formed a strategic partnership with Echeverria Izquierdo Montajes Industriales S.A. (EIMISA), a prominent Chilean firm specializing in construction and industrial services. The announcement was made on 6th May 2026. The collaboration is designed to support the delivery of mining and infrastructure projects across Chile and South America.The partnership will leverage the strengths of both companies as mining investments surge, delivering projects that meet stringent safety and quality standards. The engineering and project management expertise of the global firm will complement EIMISA's direct-hire construction throughout Chile.Dario Barros Izquierdo, CEO of EIMISA, noted that the agreement builds on a foundation of trust between the two companies, adding, "Bechtel's global expertise and technical resources, combined with our local knowledge and execution capability, create a powerful team to support Chile's growing portfolio of complex projects."

+ NABTU AND BECHTEL PARTNER TO DEVELOP THE NEXT-GENERATION NUCLEAR WORKFORCE

Bechtel and NABTU have signed an MOU to modernize apprenticeship programs for nuclear construction, as the United States pursues growth in nuclear energy including SMRs and large reactors, with the agreement signed on May 5, 2026. The agreement outlines steps to identify specialized craft skills, align curricula with industry requirements, and modernize training centers to reflect new construction methods. Leaders from Bechtel and NABTU stressed the importance of a robust training pipeline for the next generation of projects, citing Vogtle Units 3 and 4 and the Natrium Demonstration Project as benchmarks.

+ SOWELA AND BECHTEL ROLL OUT FREE PIPEFITTING PROGRAM TO BOOST LOCAL WORKFORCE

Bechtel and SOWELA Technical Community College are partnering to offer a free Pipefitting Training Program at SOWELA's Main Campus in Lake Charles, aimed at preparing locals for careers in pipefitting across manufacturing, energy, and building systems. The program runs 26th May 2026 through 13th November 2026.The curriculum covers core job duties, safety principles, and the tools and materials used in the trade, with a strong emphasis on hands-on work installing and repairing high- and low-pressure piping systems.Classes run weekdays from 8 a.m. to 3 p.m. Upon completion, participants earn OSHA 10-hour credential and are positioned to pursue NCCER Pipefitting certifications, boosting their prospects in a crowded market.A background check and drug screening are required for employment consideration. Details for registration are at www.sowela.edu/pipefitting, and inquiries can be directed to SOWELA's Office of Workforce Solutions at (337) 421-6560 or workforce@sowela.edu

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KIEWIT CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ STRATEGIC JV SECURES MAJOR O &M CENTER FOR AUSTIN LIGHT RAIL AS PART OF $7.1B PROJECT

This announcement was made on 28th April 2026. A joint venture of Kiewit Building Group and Austin Commercial has moved forward with a design-build contract to construct an operations and maintenance facility for the US$7.1 billion Austin Light Rail line. The project will support a 10-mile corridor with 15 stations and all-electric trains planned to operate every five to ten minutes.The O&M facility will store, service, and dispatch trains and provide workspace for operators, maintenance crews, and support staff.Ten design-phase subcontractors are involved, and the overall effort is expected to generate thousands of jobs during both the design and construction phases. ATP Chief Executive Greg Canally noted the project as a major step forward and highlighted the value of a locally experienced partner like Kiewit.Earlier, Aecom-led LINC Austin was named the delivery partner for the first phase, with a roster that includes Parsons, STV, Turner & Townsend, Ardmore Roderick, and CAS Consulting.

+ KAP JV CHOSEN TO LEAD LIGHT RAIL O&M FACILITY AS PROJECT CONNECT ADVANCES

The Austin Transit Partnership revealed that Kiewit Austin Partnership (KAP), a Kiewit Building Group Inc. and Austin Commercial joint venture, will design and build the Operations and Maintenance Facility, accelerating pre-construction activities. The development was announced on 21st April 2026.The 62-acre OMF site will sit on the southwest edge of the Montopolis neighborhood, near U.S. 183 and SH 71, and will store and service up to 40 light rail vehicles. Property acquisitions tied to the project will affect dozens of existing businesses, with Airport Commerce Park highlighted as an area of relocation concern.KAP’s design team already includes about 10 subcontractors, with expectations for expanded involvement as construction ramps up. The broader Project Connect program is expected to generate substantial job activity across Texas during design and construction, while the OMF itself is projected to support around 300 long-term jobs once in service. Mainline construction could begin in 2027, with service anticipated for the early 2030s, following the Federal Transit Administration's January 2026 EIS/ROD clearance for final design and land acquisition.

+ STRATEGIC PARTNERSHIP ADVANCES U.S. CLEAN HYDROGEN AND HEAVY WATER PROJECT TO PRE-FEED

Aternium has selected Kiewit Engineering Group Inc. to conduct the pre-front-end engineering and design for its first integrated hydrogen and heavy water facility, aligning with the company's plan to deploy a $1 billion program to produce these materials domestically. The announcement was made on 26th March 2026.The Mid-Atlantic site will address demand for low-carbon energy and bolster U.S. capabilities in heavy water, which is currently sourced largely from foreign suppliers. Andrew Cottone noted that the collaboration with Kiewit reflects a shared commitment to technical excellence and safety.The project combines clean hydrogen production with a patent-pending heavy water isolation process, aiming to reduce hydrogen costs and strengthen domestic supply. The pre-FEED will set the scope and design foundations for future development, positioning the venture at the crossroads of energy transition and advanced manufacturing.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION ANNOUNCES 9,100-SF INNSBROOK EXPANSION UNDER COUNTY INCENTIVES

DPR Construction plans to expand its Innsbrook office by 9,100 square feet, backed by incentives from Henrico County. The date of this announcement is 29th April 2026.The expansion is designed to accommodate a growing workforce and enhance operations in the region.Officials say the project reflects confidence in the local market and will create additional job opportunities while reinforcing the firm’s sustainability commitments.The design will feature modern elements and sustainable practices, aligning with DPR’s mission to lead in sustainable construction.Completion is anticipated within the next year.

+ DPR TAPS JASON PAGE TO LEAD NASHVILLE SPECIAL SERVICES GROUP AND EXPAND INTERIOR BUILDOUTS

The SSG handles a range of work from parking garage stair installations to interior buildouts, and under Page's leadership the group aims to meet evolving client and stakeholder needs while Page mentors teams and promotes best practices for quick-turn, high-impact work.

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION ACHIEVES GREAT PLACE TO WORK CERTIFICATION WITH 97% POSITIVE EMPLOYEE FEEDBACK

DPR Construction has earned Great Place To Work Certification after 97 percent of its employees affirmed it is a great place to work, a rate about 40 points above the national average. The certification, awarded on 31st March 2026, highlights a culture built on pride, belonging and opportunity.According to CEO George Pfeffer, the certification reflects the real sentiments of the people who carry the company’s culture. "Being Certified(TradeMark) as a great place to work is especially meaningful because it comes directly from our people, who bring our culture to life," he said, underscoring an emphasis on trust, inclusion, and growth.DPR’s approach centers on empowering employees to make decisions and share information openly across projects. This openness supports initiative, whether in innovating on complex tasks, taking on new roles, or offering ideas outside formal job descriptions, with learning and development programs backing career progression.The recognition complements DPR’s broader reputation as an employer of choice and its ongoing achievements, including industry accolades and expansion across markets. DPR, founded in 1990, has grown into a multi billion dollar contractor employing over 11,000 professionals worldwide, with innovations such as virtual design and construction and self performing craft.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ PARSONS CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ PARSONS CORPORATION (NYSE:PSN) DEPLOYS SBIR-BACKED AUTONOMOUS DRONE INITIATIVE WITH EVERYWHERE COMMUNICATIONS

Parsons Corporation (NYSE:PSN) and EVERYWHERE Communications established a strategic partnership to push autonomous drone technology under SBIR, focusing on operations in disconnected environments. The partnership was confirmed on 4th of May 2026. By leveraging Iridium Satellite technology, EVERYWHERE Communications introduces a resilient data transport layer that enables drones to operate autonomously while transmitting sensor data back to operators and command systems. The platform supports beyond-line-of-sight operations, reliable data exfiltration and autonomous mission execution, with scalable coordination for multiple drones on low-bandwidth links. Jake Bailey, President of EVERYWHERE Communications, states, "In austere environments, connectivity is never guaranteed. We're enabling drones to keep operating—and keep delivering intelligence—even when the network is compromised." Parsons contributes TAK-as-a-Service (TaaS) to provide scalable, mission-ready integration and sustainment services tailored for the Department of War and federal agencies, enhancing real-time situational awareness and data interoperability. Mike Kushin, President of Defense and Intelligence at Parsons, notes, "In an era where every second and every signal matters, this collaboration brings together resilient autonomy in the air and trusted mission systems on the ground to give our warfighters and intelligence professionals a decisive information advantage." The parties aim to redefine operational capabilities for mission-critical teams worldwide.

+ PARSONS CORPORATION (NYSE: PSN) REPORTS COLLABORATION WITH THE BORING COMPANY ON DUBAI LOOP DESIGN VERIFICATION AND PERMITTING

CHANTILLY, Va. — The Boring Company awards Parsons a nine-month contract for the Dubai Loop project, dated 5th May 2026Parsons will act as TBC’s delegated program manager, delivering Independent Design Verification, Stakeholder Management, and Permitting and NOC Support for the pilot phase, and conducting multidisciplinary reviews across civil, structural, mechanical, electrical, safety, and utility disciplinesPierre Santoni, president of Infrastructure EMEA at Parsons, said, "Parsons has proudly played a central role in developing some of the most important and prestigious critical infrastructure programs across the UAE since our first programs there in the 1980s." The partnership with TBC aligns with the UAE leadership's Dubai 2040 Urban Master Plan and aims to advance local capabilities through collaborationThe Dubai Loop project, in collaboration with Dubai’s Roads and Transit Authority (RTA), seeks to expand urban mobility through tunnels and stations. Parsons has worked with the RTA since 2005, contributing to key projects such as the Dubai Metro and various highway and bridge programsParsons has managed more than 250 tunneling projects globally and has extensive road and highway experience covering over 10,000 miles on six continents

+ PARSONS CORPORATION (NYSE:PSN) EXPANDS NYC INFRASTRUCTURE ROLE WITH NEWTOWN CREEK TUNNEL CONTRACT AND USCG JV PROGRAM MANAGEMENT

Parsons Corporation (NYSE:PSN) Selected To Manage The Newtown Creek CSO Storage Tunnel For NYC DEP Following A Contract Award On Apr. 14, 2026. The Project Calls For A Large-Diameter Storage Tunnel And A Dewatering Pump Station To Protect Local WaterwaysThe New York City Department Of Environmental Protection Cites Parsons Experience On More Than 250 Tunnel And Ocean Outfall Projects Worldwide. The Initiative Aims To Modernize Municipal Assets And Improve Water Quality Through A Technology-Driven ApproachParsons Also Forms A Joint Venture With GSI Americas To Secure A US Coast Guard Program Management Contract Worth $25 Million. The Agreement Supports The USCG Execution Plan, Including Shore Infrastructure Upgrades And Deployment Of Transformational Technologies

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
