value.Title: ZACHRY GROUP - INDUSTRY AND PEER TRENDS: THE RECENT

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHB-59C3-S8C5-13CK-00000-00ZACHRY GROUP - INDUSTRY AND PEER TRENDS: THE RECENT2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:09:33ZLexisNexisSTOCKZACHRY GROUP - INDUSTRY AND PEER TRENDS: THE RECENT

DASHBOARD: ZACHRY GROUP

Websitehttps://www.zachrygroup.com/IndustryConstrustion

ACTIVITIES:

Zachry Group, founded in 1924, is a leading construction and engineering firm headquartered in San Antonio, Texas. Employing over 20,000 people, it provides turnkey services in power, energy, chemicals, and industrial sectors, generating over $3 billion annually. Its subsidiaries, like JVIC, offer maintenance and fabrication. In 2024, Zachry expanded renewable energy projects, including solar and wind facilities. Its culture emphasizes safety, citizenship, and employee training, with a commitment to community health and ethical practices. Competing with Fluor, Zachry stands out for its EPC (engineering, procurement, construction) expertise and safety standards. Recent initiatives include digital project management tools and carbon-neutral construction goals, aligning with sustainability trends.

+ ZACHRY GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ ZACHRY GROUP EXPANDS INTO OKLAHOMA WITH ROOSEVELT BRIDGE REPLACEMENT PROJECT

Zachry Construction Corporation has won a contract to replace the US-70 Roosevelt Bridge in Oklahoma, working in a joint venture with Traylor Bros., Inc. The Oklahoma Department of Transportation selected the Zachry-Traylor JV as the delivery partner, in what is the first collaboration between Zachry Construction and the OklahomaDOT.The announcement, dated 28th April 2026, underscores a significant upgrade to a corridor that connects Bryan and Marshall counties and serves travelers headed to Lake Texoma.The project will replace the aging structure with a new four-lane crossing featuring modern amenities including bicycle and pedestrian accommodations, a median barrier, and upgraded lighting. The existing bridge will be demolished, with traffic maintained on the current structure during construction to minimize disruption.Zachry Construction brings more than a century of experience in heavy and highway work, and the Traylor Bros. team is recognized for its expertise in bridge projects. Together they aim to deliver a state-of-the-art crossing that enhances connectivity and supports regional growth. Completion is targeted for 1st October 2029.

+ ZACHRY CONSTRUCTION PROMOTES MROSS TO PRESIDENT AS WATER AND INFRASTRUCTURE GROWTH ACCELERATES

Travis Mross has been elevated to president at Zachry Construction Corp., with the transition effective 29th April 2026. The promotion signals a continuing development plan for the San Antonio-based, family-owned contractor.Beginning as an intern in 2000 and rising to executive vice president for six years, Mross helped guide the firm's strategy and improve efficiency. He takes over from David Zachry, who will devote his efforts to CEO responsibilities.Zachry Construction remains a leader in heavy civil, roadway and infrastructure work, having completed more than 6,000 projects across public and private sectors. The company is expanding in water and wastewater and recently acquired Crescent Constructors Inc. to broaden its capabilities in that area.As president, Mross will oversee water, transportation, underground utilities and civil sitework, prioritizing growth, execution discipline and a strong corporate culture. He stated, "Our job is to bring consistency, discipline, and a high standard of execution to every project, which will guide how we serve clients and grow the business."

+ ZACHRY GROUP SECURES GROUND LEASE, ADDS PARKING LOTS TO SUPPORT MONARCH HOTEL

On Apr. 29, 2026, Zachry Group, through its affiliate Zachry Park Garage QOZB LLC, leased a parking lot from St. John's Evangelical Lutheran Church for a site across South Presa Street from the church near La Villita and Hemisfair, and acquired a smaller lot from St. Johns Four Square LLC, bringing the combined area to about 1.2 acres. Those parcels were part of a larger development plan by Dennis McDaniel in partnership with Opportunity Home San Antonio for 252 apartments and retail space, a plan that stalled during the pandemic.A Zachry representative said the spaces will support operational needs for the Monarch San Antonio hotel, which opened overlooking Civic Park. There are no immediate plans for additional development on the sites.Zachry Group, founded in 1924 by Henry Bartell 'Pat' Zachry, has a long-standing presence in San Antonio and was involved in the Monarch project, a roughly $185 million development that has helped shape the city’s hospitality landscape.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BECHTEL [3 News That Matters]

+ SOWELA AND BECHTEL ROLL OUT FREE PIPEFITTING PROGRAM TO BOOST LOCAL WORKFORCE

Bechtel and SOWELA Technical Community College are partnering to offer a free Pipefitting Training Program at SOWELA's Main Campus in Lake Charles, aimed at preparing locals for careers in pipefitting across manufacturing, energy, and building systems. The program runs 26th May 2026 through 13th November 2026.The curriculum covers core job duties, safety principles, and the tools and materials used in the trade, with a strong emphasis on hands-on work installing and repairing high- and low-pressure piping systems.Classes run weekdays from 8 a.m. to 3 p.m. Upon completion, participants earn OSHA 10-hour credential and are positioned to pursue NCCER Pipefitting certifications, boosting their prospects in a crowded market.A background check and drug screening are required for employment consideration. Details for registration are at www.sowela.edu/pipefitting, and inquiries can be directed to SOWELA's Office of Workforce Solutions at (337) 421-6560 or workforce@sowela.edu

+ ALBERT FAMILY FUNDS INDUSTRY-INTEGRATED CONSTRUCTION EDUCATION AT UNC CHARLOTTE

UNC Charlotte is launching the Albert School of Construction within the William States Lee College of Engineering through a leadership gift from Craig and Darla Albert. The effort, announced Apr. 7, 2026, aims to unite engineering, business and data science to cover the full lifecycle of capital projects. Rob Keynton, dean of the college, described the program as a cohesive framework designed to prepare graduates for rapid professional impact and strong industry partnerships. Lingguang Song will lead the new school as Executive Director, bringing a background in engineering technology and construction management. The Alberts ’ initiative is the first phase of broader growth plans, including hands-on learning facilities, an integrated curriculum and endowed faculty positions, with the couple serving as co-chairs of the For the Love of Charlotte campaign targeting $500 million for the university.

+ BECHTEL ELEVATES LUCY MARTIN TO LEAD MINING & METALS DIVISION

Bechtel named Lucy Martin as president of its Mining & Metals business, effective April 6, 2026, as Ailie MacAdam plans to retire in May after 41 years with the company. Bechtel President and COO Craig Albert remarked on 3rd April 2026 that Lucy is a proven leader with the strategic vision and sector experience to advance the Mining & Metals unit, noting Martin has spent more than 20 years at Bechtel building global teams to tackle complex challenges. Martin has said she aims to build on the division's momentum amid a shifting global market, emphasizing a focus on delivering results through cost-efficient development, productivity, safety, reliability, and certainty of outcomes. MacAdam oversaw major projects in global markets and oversaw 2025’s strongest new-work bookings in 15 years for Mining & Metals. Bechtel continues to serve Energy, Infrastructure, Manufacturing & Technology, Mining & Metals, and Nuclear, and remains committed to outcomes amid an evolving energy landscape.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KIEWIT CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ STRATEGIC JV SECURES MAJOR O&M CENTER FOR AUSTIN LIGHT RAIL AS PART OF $7.1B PROJECT

This announcement was made on 28th April 2026. A joint venture of Kiewit Building Group and Austin Commercial has moved forward with a design-build contract to construct an operations and maintenance facility for the US$7.1 billion Austin Light Rail line. The project will support a 10-mile corridor with 15 stations and all-electric trains planned to operate every five to ten minutes.The O&M facility will store, service, and dispatch trains and provide workspace for operators, maintenance crews, and support staff.Ten design-phase subcontractors are involved, and the overall effort is expected to generate thousands of jobs during both the design and construction phases. ATP Chief Executive Greg Canally noted the project as a major step forward and highlighted the value of a locally experienced partner like Kiewit.Earlier, Aecom-led LINC Austin was named the delivery partner for the first phase, with a roster that includes Parsons, STV, Turner & Townsend, Ardmore Roderick, and CAS Consulting.

+ KAP JV CHOSEN TO LEAD LIGHT RAIL O&M FACILITY AS PROJECT CONNECT ADVANCES

The Austin Transit Partnership revealed that Kiewit Austin Partnership (KAP), a Kiewit Building Group Inc. and Austin Commercial joint venture, will design and build the Operations and Maintenance Facility, accelerating pre-construction activities. The development was announced on 21st April 2026.The 62-acre OMF site will sit on the southwest edge of the Montopolis neighborhood, near U.S. 183 and SH 71, and will store and service up to 40 light rail vehicles. Property acquisitions tied to the project will affect dozens of existing businesses, with Airport Commerce Park highlighted as an area of relocation concern.KAP’s design team already includes about 10 subcontractors, with expectations for expanded involvement as construction ramps up. The broader Project Connect program is expected to generate substantial job activity across Texas during design and construction, while the OMF itself is projected to support around 300 long-term jobs once in service. Mainline construction could begin in 2027, with service anticipated for the early 2030s, following the Federal Transit Administration's January 2026 EIS/ROD clearance for final design and land acquisition.

+ STRATEGIC PARTNERSHIP ADVANCES U.S. CLEAN HYDROGEN AND HEAVY WATER PROJECT TO PRE-FEED

Aternium has selected Kiewit Engineering Group Inc. to conduct the pre-front-end engineering and design for its first integrated hydrogen and heavy water facility, aligning with the company's plan to deploy a $1 billion program to produce these materials domestically. The announcement was made on 26th March 2026.The Mid-Atlantic site will address demand for low-carbon energy and bolster U.S. capabilities in heavy water, which is currently sourced largely from foreign suppliers. Andrew Cottone noted that the collaboration with Kiewit reflects a shared commitment to technical excellence and safety.The project combines clean hydrogen production with a patent-pending heavy water isolation process, aiming to reduce hydrogen costs and strengthen domestic supply. The pre-FEED will set the scope and design foundations for future development, positioning the venture at the crossroads of energy transition and advanced manufacturing.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION ANNOUNCES 9,100-SF INNSBROOK EXPANSION UNDER COUNTY INCENTIVES

DPR Construction plans to expand its Innsbrook office by 9,100 square feet, backed by incentives from Henrico County. The date of this announcement is 29th April 2026.The expansion is designed to accommodate a growing workforce and enhance operations in the region.Officials say the project reflects confidence in the local market and will create additional job opportunities while reinforcing the firm’s sustainability commitments.The design will feature modern elements and sustainable practices, aligning with DPR’s mission to lead in sustainable construction.Completion is anticipated within the next year.

+ DPR TAPS JASON PAGE TO LEAD NASHVILLE SPECIAL SERVICES GROUP AND EXPAND INTERIOR BUILDOUTS

The SSG handles a range of work from parking garage stair installations to interior buildouts, and under Page's leadership the group aims to meet evolving client and stakeholder needs while Page mentors teams and promotes best practices for quick-turn, high-impact work.

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION ACHIEVES GREAT PLACE TO WORK CERTIFICATION WITH 97% POSITIVE EMPLOYEE FEEDBACK

DPR Construction has earned Great Place To Work Certification after 97 percent of its employees affirmed it is a great place to work, a rate about 40 points above the national average. The certification, awarded on 31st March 2026, highlights a culture built on pride, belonging and opportunity.According to CEO George Pfeffer, the certification reflects the real sentiments of the people who carry the company’s culture. "Being Certified(TradeMark) as a great place to work is especially meaningful because it comes directly from our people, who bring our culture to life," he said, underscoring an emphasis on trust, inclusion, and growth.DPR’s approach centers on empowering employees to make decisions and share information openly across projects. This openness supports initiative, whether in innovating on complex tasks, taking on new roles, or offering ideas outside formal job descriptions, with learning and development programs backing career progression.The recognition complements DPR’s broader reputation as an employer of choice and its ongoing achievements, including industry accolades and expansion across markets. DPR, founded in 1990, has grown into a multi billion dollar contractor employing over 11,000 professionals worldwide, with innovations such as virtual design and construction and self performing craft.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ PARSONS CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ PARSONS CORPORATION (NYSE:PSN) EXPANDS NYC INFRASTRUCTURE ROLE WITH NEWTOWN CREEK TUNNEL CONTRACT AND USCG JV PROGRAM MANAGEMENT

Parsons Corporation (NYSE:PSN) Selected To Manage The Newtown Creek CSO Storage Tunnel For NYC DEP Following A Contract Award On Apr. 14, 2026. The Project Calls For A Large-Diameter Storage Tunnel And A Dewatering Pump Station To Protect Local WaterwaysThe New York City Department Of Environmental Protection Cites Parsons Experience On More Than 250 Tunnel And Ocean Outfall Projects Worldwide. The Initiative Aims To Modernize Municipal Assets And Improve Water Quality Through A Technology-Driven ApproachParsons Also Forms A Joint Venture With GSI Americas To Secure A US Coast Guard Program Management Contract Worth $25 Million. The Agreement Supports The USCG Execution Plan, Including Shore Infrastructure Upgrades And Deployment Of Transformational Technologies

+ PARSONS CORPORATION (NYSE: PSN) POSTS $25 MILLION US COAST GUARD PROGRAM MANAGEMENT AGREEMENT WITH GSIA

Parsons Corporation (NYSE: PSN) and its joint venture with GSI Americas Inc. (GSIA) secure a $25 million US Coast Guard program management contract, awarded on 15th April 2026, with a one-year base term and four one-year optionsThe arrangement will support the Force Design 2028 Execution Plan, a major modernization effort for the Coast Guard that includes upgrades to assets, technology, and shore infrastructure and is backed by a $24.5 billion budget for modernization, training facilities, and enhanced mission capacitiesMartin Boson, president of Engineered Systems for Parsons, remarked, "We look forward to leveraging our team’s combined global program management experience and project delivery success to support the U.S. Coast Guard in this modernization effort that strengthens readiness and builds a more agile, capable, and responsive force." Michael Warminsky, senior vice president for GSIA, said, "We are proud to support the U.S. Coast Guard’s Force Design 2028 Execution Plan alongside Parsons, contributing to a program that enhances operational readiness and strengthens national maritime security." Parsons has been recognized by Engineering News-Record for its program management capabilities, reflecting the strength of this partnershipThe collaboration aligns Parsons’ project lifecycle expertise with GSIA’s energy and infrastructure capabilities to support national security and modernization goals

+ PARSONS CORPORATION (NYSE:PSN) DESIGNS ROOSEVELT BRIDGE REPLACEMENT, SECURING $409 MILLION AWARD

The Roosevelt Bridge Replacement Project is a $409 million undertaking that will be executed in two phases to maintain traffic. Parsons will lead the design while the Zachry Construction/Traylor Brothers Construction Joint Venture handles construction. Funding includes a federal grant of $124 million awarded in 2024, complemented by state funding. Parsons stock has declined 35% over the past six months and currently trades at $56.01, with a market capitalization of approximately $6 billion. Mark Fialkowski, president of Infrastructure North America at Parsons, highlighted the company’s legacy in bridge design and its ability to deliver complex infrastructure solutions worldwide. In addition to Roosevelt, Parsons is advancing other initiatives, including a 10-year Vehicle-to-Everything Maintenance and Management System contract with the Utah Department of Transportation, a $389 million I 64 Virginia design project, and project management consulting for Saudi Arabia’s Al Ittihad Sports Village. The stock recently received a Neutral rating from Baird due to concerns about potential revenue declines in the Middle East

Past Week: Weekly: OGE Energy Corp. (NYSE:OGE) ticks lower by 1.7% on firm volume

OGE Energy Corp. (NYSE:OGE $47.35), the NYSE's 6th largest Pipeline company by market cap, has ticked lower by 83c (or 1.7%) in the past week to close at $47.35.

In the past week the market cap has declined by $170.2 million to $9.7 billion.

The volume was 1.1 times average trading per week of 6.5 million shares.

Activities

OGE Energy Corp., a leading energy services holding company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, operates primarily through its subsidiary, Oklahoma Gas and Electric Company (OG&E), to provide reliable electric utility services. The company's core activities include electricity generation, transmission, and distribution to customers in Oklahoma and western Arkansas, with a focus on maintaining a resilient infrastructure and adhering to regulatory standards. In recent years, OGE's fastest-growing segments have been its renewable energy initiatives, such as wind and solar power projects, driven by increasing demand for sustainable energy and supportive policies. Additionally, investments in smart grid technologies and grid modernization efforts are accelerating, enhancing operational efficiency and enabling better integration of clean energy sources. This strategic emphasis positions OGE to capitalize on the evolving energy market while ensuring long-term value for stakeholders. It is the NYSE's 6th largest Pipeline company by market capitalisation.

OGE Energy director awarded Stock Equivalent Units recently, reflecting an integral element of OGE Energy director compensation. The OGE Energy stock equivalent units result in an OGE Energy stock award that aligns with strategic goals and shareholder interests. Through OGE Energy Corp stock grant operations, directors receive an OGE Energy equity award, further supplemented by OGE Energy restricted stock units and OGE Energy deferred stock units. The announcement of OGE Energy insider awards is detailed in the OGE Energy Form 4 filing, representation of transparent OGE Energy corporate governance stock awards.

Further insights into OGE Energy board director stock grant and OGE Energy beneficial ownership are revealed in the OGE Energy proxy statement stock awards. The OGE Energy executive compensation framework underpins director RSU award and adheres to company values, outlined in the OGE Energy shareholder disclosure and OGE Energy SEC filing equity grant documents. OGE Energy insider trading disclosure ensures transparency across OGE Energy board compensation, aligning with long-term plans and OGE Energy equity compensation plan commitments.

Past Week: OGE Energy Corp. (NYSE:OGE) shares post worst week in twenty-two weeks

OGE Energy Corp. (NYSE:OGE $47.33), the NYSE's 4th largest Pipeline company by market cap, has decreased $1.47 (or 3.0%) in the past week to close at $47.33.

The shares had their worst week in twenty-two weeks since the week-ended Dec 11, 2025.

In the past week the market cap has declined by $301.4 million to $9.7 billion.

Activities

OGE Energy Corp., a leading energy services holding company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, operates primarily through its subsidiary, Oklahoma Gas and Electric Company (OG&E), to provide reliable electric utility services. The company's core activities include electricity generation, transmission, and distribution to customers in Oklahoma and western Arkansas, with a focus on maintaining a resilient infrastructure and adhering to regulatory standards. In recent years, OGE's fastest-growing segments have been its renewable energy initiatives, such as wind and solar power projects, driven by increasing demand for sustainable energy and supportive policies. Additionally, investments in smart grid technologies and grid modernization efforts are accelerating, enhancing operational efficiency and enabling better integration of clean energy sources. This strategic emphasis positions OGE to capitalize on the evolving energy market while ensuring long-term value for stakeholders. It is the NYSE's 4th largest Pipeline company by market capitalisation.

OGE Energy director awarded Stock Equivalent Units recently, reflecting an integral element of OGE Energy director compensation. The OGE Energy stock equivalent units result in an OGE Energy stock award that aligns with strategic goals and shareholder interests. Through OGE Energy Corp stock grant operations, directors receive an OGE Energy equity award, further supplemented by OGE Energy restricted stock units and OGE Energy deferred stock units. The announcement of OGE Energy insider awards is detailed in the OGE Energy Form 4 filing, representation of transparent OGE Energy corporate governance stock awards.

Further insights into OGE Energy board director stock grant and OGE Energy beneficial ownership are revealed in the OGE Energy proxy statement stock awards. The OGE Energy executive compensation framework underpins director RSU award and adheres to company values, outlined in the OGE Energy shareholder disclosure and OGE Energy SEC filing equity grant documents. OGE Energy insider trading disclosure ensures transparency across OGE Energy board compensation, aligning with long-term plans and OGE Energy equity compensation plan commitments.

urn:contentItem:6JHM-RY53-RVFD-P034-00000-00Hitachi Ltd (NYSE:HTHIY (ADR)) Forms Memorandum Of Cooperation With Oklahoma At SelectUSA Investment Summit2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:09:33ZLexisNexisHitachi Ltd (NYSE:HTHIY (ADR)) Forms Memorandum Of Cooperation With Oklahoma At SelectUSA Investment Summit

DAILY ADR AND DEPOSITORY RECEIPT REPORT

UNITED STATES EDITION

06 May 2026 14:31 EDT

Hitachi Ltd (NYSE:HTHIY (ADR)) Forms Memorandum Of Cooperation With Oklahoma At SelectUSA Investment Summit To Establish A Framework For Collaboration In Energy Infrastructure, Advanced Manufacturing And Digital Solutions. The Agreement, Signed May 4, 2026, Highlights The Ongoing Public-Private Partnership Between The State And Hitachi, Which Has Operated In Oklahoma For Nearly 40 Years And Employs More Than 200 People In Norman.

Governor Kevin Stitt Emphasized The Value Of Such Alliances, Saying The State Seeks To Connect Oklahoma With Global Markets, And Hitachi Exemplifies Foreign Direct Investment Combined With Public-Private Partnership To Create Shared Success. Charlie Takeuchi, President And CEO Of Hitachi Americas, Described The Partnership As A Path To Sustainable Growth And Long-Term Economic Value For Oklahoma.

In Addition To The Memorandum Of Cooperation, Hitachi Has Engaged With Oklahoma Through The Business Expansion Incentive Program (BEIP), Committing $4 Million For New Machinery And Equipment To Expand Operational Capacity While Reducing Energy And Maintenance Costs. The Collaboration Aligns With Oklahoma’s Strategy To Attract Foreign Direct Investment And Foster A Pro-Business Environment, As Highlighted By John Budd, CEO Of The Oklahoma Department Of Commerce. The Partnership Is Expected To Strengthen Local Innovation And Position Oklahoma As An Attractive Destination For Global Businesses Seeking Growth Opportunities.

SECTION 1 HITACHI ACTIVITIES

SECTION 1 Hitachi Activities

Activities

Hitachi Limited, a Tokyo-based multinational conglomerate founded in 1910, is a global leader in digital solutions, infrastructure, and technology-driven industries. The company's core business activities span information technology services, power systems, rail and urban transportation, construction machinery, and healthcare equipment, leveraging its expertise to deliver innovative, sustainable solutions worldwide. Hitachi's fastest-growing segments include digital transformation through its Lumada platform, which integrates IoT, AI, and data analytics to enhance operational efficiency for clients; renewable energy solutions, such as advanced energy storage and grid management systems; and electrification technologies in transportation and industrial automation. These areas have driven significant revenue growth amid rising demand for green technologies and smart infrastructure, positioning Hitachi as a key player in the global shift toward digital and sustainable economies. With a strong presence in over 100 countries, the company continues to invest in R&D to maintain its competitive edge. It is the International Depository Receipt (IDR) market's 5th largest Electrical component & equipment company by market capitalisation.

SECTION 2 CORPORATE PROFILE

2.1 Activities

OGE Energy Corp., a leading energy services holding company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, operates primarily through its subsidiary, Oklahoma Gas and Electric Company (OG&E), to provide reliable electric utility services. The company's core activities include electricity generation, transmission, and distribution to customers in Oklahoma and western Arkansas, with a focus on maintaining a resilient infrastructure and adhering to regulatory standards. In recent years, OGE's fastest-growing segments have been its renewable energy initiatives, such as wind and solar power projects, driven by increasing demand for sustainable energy and supportive policies. Additionally, investments in smart grid technologies and grid modernization efforts are accelerating, enhancing operational efficiency and enabling better integration of clean energy sources. This strategic emphasis positions OGE to capitalize on the evolving energy market while ensuring long-term value for stakeholders. It is the NYSE's 7th largest Pipeline company by market capitalisation.

OGE Energy director awarded Stock Equivalent Units recently, reflecting an integral element of OGE Energy director compensation. The OGE Energy stock equivalent units result in an OGE Energy stock award that aligns with strategic goals and shareholder interests. Through OGE Energy Corp stock grant operations, directors receive an OGE Energy equity award, further supplemented by OGE Energy restricted stock units and OGE Energy deferred stock units. The announcement of OGE Energy insider awards is detailed in the OGE Energy Form 4 filing, representation of transparent OGE Energy corporate governance stock awards.

Further insights into OGE Energy board director stock grant and OGE Energy beneficial ownership are revealed in the OGE Energy proxy statement stock awards. The OGE Energy executive compensation framework underpins director RSU award and adheres to company values, outlined in the OGE Energy shareholder disclosure and OGE Energy SEC filing equity grant documents. OGE Energy insider trading disclosure ensures transparency across OGE Energy board compensation, aligning with long-term plans and OGE Energy equity compensation plan commitments.

SECTION 3 PAST YEAR PRESS RELEASES

3.1 Press Releases and Corporate Wire

Press Release article 1 of 5, 253 words

March 13: OGE Energy : Outlook

2026 Outlook

Key assumptions for the 2026 outlook are discussed below.

OGE Energy is projected to earn approximately $494 million to $514 million, or $2.38 to $2.48 per average diluted share, with a midpoint of approximately $504 million, or $2.43 per average diluted share in 2026 and is based off the following assumptions:

OGE Energy forecasts earnings for OG&E of approximately $533 million, or $2.57 per average diluted share; OGE Energy forecasts a loss of approximately $30 million for other operations (primarily the holding company), or a loss of $0.14 per average diluted share; OG&E experiences normal weather patterns for the year; OG&E has significant seasonality in its earnings; OG&E typically shows minimal earnings in the first and fourth quarters with a majority of its earnings in the third quarter due to the seasonal nature of air conditioning demand; total retail load growth of approximately 4 to 6 percent; operating expenses of approximately $1.228 billion to $1.238 billion, with operation and maintenance expenses comprising approximately 45 percent of the total; net interest expense of approximately $256 million to $261 million which assumes a $14 million allowance for borrowed funds used during construction reduction to interest expense, and assumes a debt issuance at OG&E of approximately $300 million; other income of approximately $24 million including $26 million of allowance for equity funds used during construction; an effective consolidated tax rate of approximately 16.8 percent; and approximately 207.3 million average diluted shares outstanding.

Press Release article 4 of 5, 292 words

December 31 2025: OGE Energy: File SEC Form 10-K - Management's Discussion and Analysis 10-K

Management's Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations.

The following combined discussion is separately filed by OGE Energy and OG&E. However, OG&E does not make any representations as to information related solely to OGE Energy or the subsidiaries of OGE Energy other than itself.

Overview

OGE Energy is a holding company whose primary investment provides electricity in Oklahoma and western Arkansas. OGE Energy's electric company operations are conducted through its wholly-owned subsidiary, OG&E, which generates, transmits, distributes and sells electric energy in Oklahoma and western Arkansas and are reported through OGE Energy's electric company business segment. OG&E's rates are subject to regulation by the OCC, the APSC and the FERC. OG&E was incorporated in 1902 under the laws of the Oklahoma Territory and is the largest electric company in Oklahoma, with a franchised service territory that includes Fort Smith, Arkansas and the surrounding communities. OG&E sold its retail natural gas business in 1928 and is no longer engaged in the natural gas distribution business.

The accounts of OGE Energy and its wholly-owned subsidiaries, including OG&E, are included in OGE Energy's consolidated financial statements. All intercompany transactions and balances are eliminated in such consolidation.

Recent Developments

OG&E's Regulatory Matters

On March 27, 2025, the OCC issued a final order approving the settlement agreement relating to OG&E's 2023 general rate review and approved the ALJ report on the remaining one MW issue with one exception. OG&E also issued its 2025 IRP to the OCC and APSC and has filed for and/or received preapproval, in both Oklahoma and Arkansas, of certain generation and capacity investments identified by OG&E's IRP and related request for proposals process.

SECTION 2 CORPORATE PROFILE

2.1 Activities

OGE Energy Corp., a leading energy services holding company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, operates primarily through its subsidiary, Oklahoma Gas and Electric Company (OG&E), to provide reliable electric utility services. The company's core activities include electricity generation, transmission, and distribution to customers in Oklahoma and western Arkansas, with a focus on maintaining a resilient infrastructure and adhering to regulatory standards. In recent years, OGE's fastest-growing segments have been its renewable energy initiatives, such as wind and solar power projects, driven by increasing demand for sustainable energy and supportive policies. Additionally, investments in smart grid technologies and grid modernization efforts are accelerating, enhancing operational efficiency and enabling better integration of clean energy sources. This strategic emphasis positions OGE to capitalize on the evolving energy market while ensuring long-term value for stakeholders. It is the NYSE's 6th largest Pipeline company by market capitalisation.

OGE Energy director awarded Stock Equivalent Units recently, reflecting an integral element of OGE Energy director compensation. The OGE Energy stock equivalent units result in an OGE Energy stock award that aligns with strategic goals and shareholder interests. Through OGE Energy Corp stock grant operations, directors receive an OGE Energy equity award, further supplemented by OGE Energy restricted stock units and OGE Energy deferred stock units. The announcement of OGE Energy insider awards is detailed in the OGE Energy Form 4 filing, representation of transparent OGE Energy corporate governance stock awards.

Further insights into OGE Energy board director stock grant and OGE Energy beneficial ownership are revealed in the OGE Energy proxy statement stock awards. The OGE Energy executive compensation framework underpins director RSU award and adheres to company values, outlined in the OGE Energy shareholder disclosure and OGE Energy SEC filing equity grant documents. OGE Energy insider trading disclosure ensures transparency across OGE Energy board compensation, aligning with long-term plans and OGE Energy equity compensation plan commitments.

March 13: OGE Energy : Outlook

2026 Outlook

Key assumptions for the 2026 outlook are discussed below.

OGE Energy is projected to earn approximately $494 million to $514 million, or $2.38 to $2.48 per average diluted share, with a midpoint of approximately $504 million, or $2.43 per average diluted share in 2026 and is based off the following assumptions:

OGE Energy forecasts earnings for OG&E of approximately $533 million, or $2.57 per average diluted share; OGE Energy forecasts a loss of approximately $30 million for other operations (primarily the holding company), or a loss of $0.14 per average diluted share; OG&E experiences normal weather patterns for the year; OG&E has significant seasonality in its earnings; OG&E typically shows minimal earnings in the first and fourth quarters with a majority of its earnings in the third quarter due to the seasonal nature of air conditioning demand; total retail load growth of approximately 4 to 6 percent; operating expenses of approximately $1.228 billion to $1.238 billion, with operation and maintenance expenses comprising approximately 45 percent of the total; net interest expense of approximately $256 million to $261 million which assumes a $14 million allowance for borrowed funds used during construction reduction to interest expense, and assumes a debt issuance at OG&E of approximately $300 million; other income of approximately $24 million including $26 million of allowance for equity funds used during construction; an effective consolidated tax rate of approximately 16.8 percent; and approximately 207.3 million average diluted shares outstanding.

Press Release article 4 of 5, 292 words

December 31 2025: OGE Energy: File SEC Form 10-K - Management's Discussion and Analysis 10-K

Management's Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations.

The following combined discussion is separately filed by OGE Energy and OG&E. However, OG&E does not make any representations as to information related solely to OGE Energy or the subsidiaries of OGE Energy other than itself.

Overview

OGE Energy is a holding company whose primary investment provides electricity in Oklahoma and western Arkansas. OGE Energy's electric company operations are conducted through its wholly-owned subsidiary, OG&E, which generates, transmits, distributes and sells electric energy in Oklahoma and western Arkansas and are reported through OGE Energy's electric company business segment. OG&E's rates are subject to regulation by the OCC, the APSC and the FERC. OG&E was incorporated in 1902 under the laws of the Oklahoma Territory and is the largest electric company in Oklahoma, with a franchised service territory that includes Fort Smith, Arkansas and the surrounding communities. OG&E sold its retail natural gas business in 1928 and is no longer engaged in the natural gas distribution business.

The accounts of OGE Energy and its wholly-owned subsidiaries, including OG&E, are included in OGE Energy's consolidated financial statements. All intercompany transactions and balances are eliminated in such consolidation.

Recent Developments

OG&E's Regulatory Matters

On March 27, 2025, the OCC issued a final order approving the settlement agreement relating to OG&E's 2023 general rate review and approved the ALJ report on the remaining one MW issue with one exception. OG&E also issued its 2025 IRP to the OCC and APSC and has filed for and/or received preapproval, in both Oklahoma and Arkansas, of certain generation and capacity investments identified by OG&E's IRP and related request for proposals process.

SECTION 2 CORPORATE PROFILE

2.1 Activities

OGE Energy Corp., a leading energy services holding company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, operates primarily through its subsidiary, Oklahoma Gas and Electric Company (OG&E), to provide reliable electric utility services. The company's core activities include electricity generation, transmission, and distribution to customers in Oklahoma and western Arkansas, with a focus on maintaining a resilient infrastructure and adhering to regulatory standards. In recent years, OGE's fastest-growing segments have been its renewable energy initiatives, such as wind and solar power projects, driven by increasing demand for sustainable energy and supportive policies. Additionally, investments in smart grid technologies and grid modernization efforts are accelerating, enhancing operational efficiency and enabling better integration of clean energy sources. This strategic emphasis positions OGE to capitalize on the evolving energy market while ensuring long-term value for stakeholders. It is the NYSE's 5th largest Pipeline company by market capitalisation.

OGE Energy director awarded Stock Equivalent Units recently, reflecting an integral element of OGE Energy director compensation. The OGE Energy stock equivalent units result in an OGE Energy stock award that aligns with strategic goals and shareholder interests. Through OGE Energy Corp stock grant operations, directors receive an OGE Energy equity award, further supplemented by OGE Energy restricted stock units and OGE Energy deferred stock units. The announcement of OGE Energy insider awards is detailed in the OGE Energy Form 4 filing, representation of transparent OGE Energy corporate governance stock awards.

Further insights into OGE Energy board director stock grant and OGE Energy beneficial ownership are revealed in the OGE Energy proxy statement stock awards. The OGE Energy executive compensation framework underpins director RSU award and adheres to company values, outlined in the OGE Energy shareholder disclosure and OGE Energy SEC filing equity grant documents. OGE Energy insider trading disclosure ensures transparency across OGE Energy board compensation, aligning with long-term plans and OGE Energy equity compensation plan commitments.

March 13: OGE Energy : Outlook

2026 Outlook

Key assumptions for the 2026 outlook are discussed below.

OGE Energy is projected to earn approximately $494 million to $514 million, or $2.38 to $2.48 per average diluted share, with a midpoint of approximately $504 million, or $2.43 per average diluted share in 2026 and is based off the following assumptions:

OGE Energy forecasts earnings for OG&E of approximately $533 million, or $2.57 per average diluted share; OGE Energy forecasts a loss of approximately $30 million for other operations (primarily the holding company), or a loss of $0.14 per average diluted share; OG&E experiences normal weather patterns for the year; OG&E has significant seasonality in its earnings; OG&E typically shows minimal earnings in the first and fourth quarters with a majority of its earnings in the third quarter due to the seasonal nature of air conditioning demand; total retail load growth of approximately 4 to 6 percent; operating expenses of approximately $1.228 billion to $1.238 billion, with operation and maintenance expenses comprising approximately 45 percent of the total; net interest expense of approximately $256 million to $261 million which assumes a $14 million allowance for borrowed funds used during construction reduction to interest expense, and assumes a debt issuance at OG&E of approximately $300 million; other income of approximately $24 million including $26 million of allowance for equity funds used during construction; an effective consolidated tax rate of approximately 16.8 percent; and approximately 207.3 million average diluted shares outstanding.

Press Release article 4 of 5, 292 words

December 31 2025: OGE Energy: File SEC Form 10-K - Management's Discussion and Analysis 10-K

Management's Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations.

The following combined discussion is separately filed by OGE Energy and OG&E. However, OG&E does not make any representations as to information related solely to OGE Energy or the subsidiaries of OGE Energy other than itself.

Overview

OGE Energy is a holding company whose primary investment provides electricity in Oklahoma and western Arkansas. OGE Energy's electric company operations are conducted through its wholly-owned subsidiary, OG&E, which generates, transmits, distributes and sells electric energy in Oklahoma and western Arkansas and are reported through OGE Energy's electric company business segment. OG&E's rates are subject to regulation by the OCC, the APSC and the FERC. OG&E was incorporated in 1902 under the laws of the Oklahoma Territory and is the largest electric company in Oklahoma, with a franchised service territory that includes Fort Smith, Arkansas and the surrounding communities. OG&E sold its retail natural gas business in 1928 and is no longer engaged in the natural gas distribution business.

The accounts of OGE Energy and its wholly-owned subsidiaries, including OG&E, are included in OGE Energy's consolidated financial statements. All intercompany transactions and balances are eliminated in such consolidation.

Recent Developments

OG&E's Regulatory Matters

On March 27, 2025, the OCC issued a final order approving the settlement agreement relating to OG&E's 2023 general rate review and approved the ALJ report on the remaining one MW issue with one exception. OG&E also issued its 2025 IRP to the OCC and APSC and has filed for and/or received preapproval, in both Oklahoma and Arkansas, of certain generation and capacity investments identified by OG&E's IRP and related request for proposals process.

SECTION 4 RECENT NEWS

Recent News 1 of 1, Source: HourlyBusinessNews

May 06 2026: OGE Energy Corp. Sees Growth Amid Infrastructure Expansion

OGE Energy Corp. is making significant strides in its growth trajectory, driven by targeted investments in infrastructure modernization and an emphasis on renewable energy. As of 6th May 2026, the company's commitment to carbon reduction and a robust renewable energy portfolio is expected to enhance its long-term growth prospects.

The utility is experiencing robust customer growth in its primary service areas of Oklahoma and Arkansas. Population increases, economic development, and rising electricity demand across residential, commercial, and industrial sectors are expanding OGE Energy’s customer base. This consistent demand growth supports higher electricity sales and creates avenues for further investments in transmission, distribution, and grid modernization projects.

To support future growth, OGE Energy is leveraging a diversified and expanding renewable energy portfolio. The company is actively increasing its use of renewable resources such as wind and solar power while ensuring reliable conventional generation for grid stability. OGE Energy plans to invest $7.29 billion between 2026 and 2030, with a target of bringing 1.7 GW of new capacity online by 2029 and an additional 1.8 GW by 2032, reflecting a long-term strategy to meet escalating demand and bolster future growth.

However, OGE Energy faces challenges, including supply-chain disruptions, rising fuel prices, and inflation, which have led to project delays and increased cost pressures. These factors have resulted in raw material inflation and logistical challenges that could continue to hinder construction activities and equipment deliveries related to the company's capital projects. Rising production costs due to increased electricity production expenses further complicate the landscape for OGE Energy.

As the demand for electricity continues to rise, OGE Energy recognizes the necessity of ongoing investment in infrastructure. Higher temperatures are straining power systems, increasing risks of equipment failures and overheating. Like OGE, other utilities such as Entergy Corporation and Dominion Energy are also prioritizing infrastructure upgrades to meet customer expectations and maintain reliable service, with substantial capital investments planned for the upcoming years.

urn:contentItem:6JHC-T733-RYT0-Y4VX-00000-00Oklahoma and Hitachi Forge Partnership to Enhance Economic Landscape2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:09:33ZLexisNexisOklahoma and Hitachi Forge Partnership to Enhance Economic Landscape

DAILY ADR AND DEPOSITORY RECEIPT REPORT

UNITED STATES EDITION

06 May 2026 08:00 EDT

Oklahoma's commitment to economic growth received a significant boost with the formalization of a strategic partnership with Hitachi Ltd. The announcement was made at the U.S. Department of Commerce's SelectUSA Investment Summit, marking a collaborative effort to explore opportunities that will deliver meaningful economic benefits to the state. This development comes on the 4th of May, 2026, a date underscoring the ongoing evolution of public-private partnerships in Oklahoma.

Governor Kevin Stitt emphasized the importance of such alliances, stating, "I always talk about bringing Oklahoma to the world and the world to Oklahoma. Hitachi is a perfect example of foreign direct investment and public-private partnerships coming together to create shared success." With nearly 40 years of presence in Oklahoma and over 200 employees based in Norman, Hitachi has demonstrated a long-standing commitment to the region's economic landscape.

The newly signed Memorandum of Cooperation (MOC) outlines a framework for Hitachi to collaborate with Oklahoma in key sectors such as energy infrastructure, advanced manufacturing, and digital solutions. Hitachi's expertise in integrating operational and information technologies is expected to play a vital role in advancing these initiatives. Charlie Takeuchi, President and CEO of Hitachi Americas, articulated the company's ambition to support sustainable growth and long-term economic value for the state through this partnership.

In addition to the MOC, Hitachi recently engaged with Oklahoma through the Business Expansion Incentive Program (BEIP), committing $4 million for new machinery and equipment. This investment is aimed at expanding operational capacity while simultaneously reducing energy and maintenance costs. The collaboration aligns with Oklahoma's strategy to attract foreign direct investment and foster a pro-business environment, as highlighted by John Budd, CEO of the Oklahoma Department of Commerce.

As Oklahoma continues to cultivate a business-friendly climate supported by robust infrastructure, partnerships such as the one with Hitachi signal a promising future for the state's economic development. The ongoing collaboration is anticipated to not only enhance local innovation but also solidify Oklahoma's position as an attractive destination for global businesses seeking growth opportunities.

IN TODAY'S REPORT:

SECTION 1 HITACHI ACTIVITIES

SECTION 1 Hitachi Activities

Activities

Hitachi Limited, a Tokyo-based multinational conglomerate founded in 1910, is a global leader in digital solutions, infrastructure, and technology-driven industries. The company's core business activities span information technology services, power systems, rail and urban transportation, construction machinery, and healthcare equipment, leveraging its expertise to deliver innovative, sustainable solutions worldwide. Hitachi's fastest-growing segments include digital transformation through its Lumada platform, which integrates IoT, AI, and data analytics to enhance operational efficiency for clients; renewable energy solutions, such as advanced energy storage and grid management systems; and electrification technologies in transportation and industrial automation. These areas have driven significant revenue growth amid rising demand for green technologies and smart infrastructure, positioning Hitachi as a key player in the global shift toward digital and sustainable economies. With a strong presence in over 100 countries, the company continues to invest in R&D to maintain its competitive edge. It is the International Depository Receipt (IDR) market's 5th largest Electrical component & equipment company by market capitalisation.

May 12th, 2026 ( Zolmax.com - Delivered by Newstex )

Tesla, IREN, and Coterra Energy are the three Energy stocks to watch today, according to MarketBeat's stock screener tool. Energy stocks are shares of companies involved in producing, refining, transporting, or supplying energy, such as oil, natural gas, coal, and renewable power. For stock market investors, this sector includes businesses like major oil producers, pipeline operators, utility companies, and clean energy firms, and their performance is often influenced by commodity prices, regulation, and global demand for energy. These companies had the highest dollar trading volume of any Energy stocks within the last several days.

Tesla (TSLA)

Tesla, Inc. designs, develops, manufactures, leases, and sells electric vehicles, and energy generation and storage systems in the United States, China, and internationally. The company operates in two segments, Automotive, and Energy Generation and Storage. The Automotive segment offers electric vehicles, as well as sells automotive regulatory credits; and non-warranty after-sales vehicle, used vehicles, body shop and parts, supercharging, retail merchandise, and vehicle insurance services.

Read Our Latest Research Report on TSLA

IREN (IREN)

IREN Limited, formerly known as Iris Energy Limited, owns and operates bitcoin mining data centers. The company was incorporated in 2018 and is headquartered in Sydney, Australia.

Read Our Latest Research Report on IREN

Coterra Energy (CTRA)

Coterra Energy Inc., an independent oil and gas company, engages in the development, exploration, and production of oil, natural gas, and natural gas liquids in the United States. The company's properties include the Marcellus Shale with approximately 186,000 net acres in the dry gas window of the play located in Susquehanna County, Pennsylvania; Permian Basin properties with approximately 296,000 net acres located in westTexas and southeast New Mexico; and Anadarko Basin properties with approximately 182,000 net acres located in Oklahoma.

Read Our Latest Research Report on CTRA

The Automotive segment offers electric vehicles, as well as sells automotive regulatory credits; and non-warranty after-sales vehicle, used vehicles, body shop and parts, supercharging, retail merchandise, and vehicle insurance services.

Read Our Latest Research Report on TSLA

IREN (IREN)

IREN Limited, formerly known as Iris Energy Limited, owns and operates bitcoin mining data centers. The company was incorporated in 2018 and is headquartered in Sydney, Australia.

Read Our Latest Research Report on IREN

Coterra Energy (CTRA)

Coterra Energy Inc., an independent oil and gas company, engages in the development, exploration, and production of oil, natural gas, and natural gas liquids in the United States. The company's properties include the Marcellus Shale with approximately 186,000 net acres in the dry gas window of the play located in Susquehanna County, Pennsylvania; Permian Basin properties with approximately 296,000 net acres located in westTexas and southeast New Mexico; and Anadarko Basin properties with approximately 182,000 net acres located in Oklahoma.

SECTION 2 CORPORATE PROFILE

2.1 Activities

OGE Energy Corp., a leading energy services holding company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, operates primarily through its subsidiary, Oklahoma Gas and Electric Company (OG&E), to provide reliable electric utility services. The company's core activities include electricity generation, transmission, and distribution to customers in Oklahoma and western Arkansas, with a focus on maintaining a resilient infrastructure and adhering to regulatory standards. In recent years, OGE's fastest-growing segments have been its renewable energy initiatives, such as wind and solar power projects, driven by increasing demand for sustainable energy and supportive policies. Additionally, investments in smart grid technologies and grid modernization efforts are accelerating, enhancing operational efficiency and enabling better integration of clean energy sources. This strategic emphasis positions OGE to capitalize on the evolving energy market while ensuring long-term value for stakeholders. It is the NYSE's 4th largest Pipeline company by market capitalisation.

OGE Energy director awarded Stock Equivalent Units recently, reflecting an integral element of OGE Energy director compensation. The OGE Energy stock equivalent units result in an OGE Energy stock award that aligns with strategic goals and shareholder interests. Through OGE Energy Corp stock grant operations, directors receive an OGE Energy equity award, further supplemented by OGE Energy restricted stock units and OGE Energy deferred stock units. The announcement of OGE Energy insider awards is detailed in the OGE Energy Form 4 filing, representation of transparent OGE Energy corporate governance stock awards.

Further insights into OGE Energy board director stock grant and OGE Energy beneficial ownership are revealed in the OGE Energy proxy statement stock awards. The OGE Energy executive compensation framework underpins director RSU award and adheres to company values, outlined in the OGE Energy shareholder disclosure and OGE Energy SEC filing equity grant documents. OGE Energy insider trading disclosure ensures transparency across OGE Energy board compensation, aligning with long-term plans and OGE Energy equity compensation plan commitments.

March 13: OGE Energy : Outlook

2026 Outlook

Key assumptions for the 2026 outlook are discussed below.

OGE Energy is projected to earn approximately $494 million to $514 million, or $2.38 to $2.48 per average diluted share, with a midpoint of approximately $504 million, or $2.43 per average diluted share in 2026 and is based off the following assumptions:

OGE Energy forecasts earnings for OG&E of approximately $533 million, or $2.57 per average diluted share; OGE Energy forecasts a loss of approximately $30 million for other operations (primarily the holding company), or a loss of $0.14 per average diluted share; OG&E experiences normal weather patterns for the year; OG&E has significant seasonality in its earnings; OG&E typically shows minimal earnings in the first and fourth quarters with a majority of its earnings in the third quarter due to the seasonal nature of air conditioning demand; total retail load growth of approximately 4 to 6 percent; operating expenses of approximately $1.228 billion to $1.238 billion, with operation and maintenance expenses comprising approximately 45 percent of the total; net interest expense of approximately $256 million to $261 million which assumes a $14 million allowance for borrowed funds used during construction reduction to interest expense, and assumes a debt issuance at OG&E of approximately $300 million; other income of approximately $24 million including $26 million of allowance for equity funds used during construction; an effective consolidated tax rate of approximately 16.8 percent; and approximately 207.3 million average diluted shares outstanding.

Press Release article 4 of 5, 292 words

December 31 2025: OGE Energy: File SEC Form 10-K - Management's Discussion and Analysis 10-K

Management's Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations.

The following combined discussion is separately filed by OGE Energy and OG&E. However, OG&E does not make any representations as to information related solely to OGE Energy or the subsidiaries of OGE Energy other than itself.

Overview

OGE Energy is a holding company whose primary investment provides electricity in Oklahoma and western Arkansas. OGE Energy's electric company operations are conducted through its wholly-owned subsidiary, OG&E, which generates, transmits, distributes and sells electric energy in Oklahoma and western Arkansas and are reported through OGE Energy's electric company business segment. OG&E's rates are subject to regulation by the OCC, the APSC and the FERC. OG&E was incorporated in 1902 under the laws of the Oklahoma Territory and is the largest electric company in Oklahoma, with a franchised service territory that includes Fort Smith, Arkansas and the surrounding communities. OG&E sold its retail natural gas business in 1928 and is no longer engaged in the natural gas distribution business.

The accounts of OGE Energy and its wholly-owned subsidiaries, including OG&E, are included in OGE Energy's consolidated financial statements. All intercompany transactions and balances are eliminated in such consolidation.

Recent Developments

OG&E's Regulatory Matters

On March 27, 2025, the OCC issued a final order approving the settlement agreement relating to OG&E's 2023 general rate review and approved the ALJ report on the remaining one MW issue with one exception. OG&E also issued its 2025 IRP to the OCC and APSC and has filed for and/or received preapproval, in both Oklahoma and Arkansas, of certain generation and capacity investments identified by OG&E's IRP and related request for proposals process.

Press Release article 5 of 5, Source: WEC, 79 words

OGE Energy today announced a quarterly dividend of 42.50c per share for Q4/2025; unchanged from the previous quarter (Q3/2025) dividend of 42.50c. The ex-dividend date is Monday, January 05, 2026 and the record date is Monday, January 05, 2026 and it is payable on Friday, January 30, 2026. Total dividends per share paid in the 12 months ended December 05 were $1.69.

SECTION 4 RECENT NEWS

Recent News 1 of 2, Source: HourlyBusinessNews

May 06 2026: OGE Energy Corp. Sees Growth Amid Infrastructure Expansion

OGE Energy Corp. is making significant strides in its growth trajectory, driven by targeted investments in infrastructure modernization and an emphasis on renewable energy. As of 6th May 2026, the company's commitment to carbon reduction and a robust renewable energy portfolio is expected to enhance its long-term growth prospects.

The utility is experiencing robust customer growth in its primary service areas of Oklahoma and Arkansas. Population increases, economic development, and rising electricity demand across residential, commercial, and industrial sectors are expanding OGE Energy’s customer base. This consistent demand growth supports higher electricity sales and creates avenues for further investments in transmission, distribution, and grid modernization projects.

To support future growth, OGE Energy is leveraging a diversified and expanding renewable energy portfolio. The company is actively increasing its use of renewable resources such as wind and solar power while ensuring reliable conventional generation for grid stability. OGE Energy plans to invest $7.29 billion between 2026 and 2030, with a target of bringing 1.7 GW of new capacity online by 2029 and an additional 1.8 GW by 2032, reflecting a long-term strategy to meet escalating demand and bolster future growth.

However, OGE Energy faces challenges, including supply-chain disruptions, rising fuel prices, and inflation, which have led to project delays and increased cost pressures. These factors have resulted in raw material inflation and logistical challenges that could continue to hinder construction activities and equipment deliveries related to the company's capital projects. Rising production costs due to increased electricity production expenses further complicate the landscape for OGE Energy.

As the demand for electricity continues to rise, OGE Energy recognizes the necessity of ongoing investment in infrastructure. Higher temperatures are straining power systems, increasing risks of equipment failures and overheating. Like OGE, other utilities such as Entergy Corporation and Dominion Energy are also prioritizing infrastructure upgrades to meet customer expectations and maintain reliable service, with substantial capital investments planned for the upcoming years.

Recent News 2 of 2, Source: HourlyBusinessNews

May 06 2026: OGE Energy Corp. Secures Major Google Data Center Deal

Utility stocks are often seen as steady but unexciting investments, characterized by their slow growth and patience-driven returns. However, OGE Energy Corp. has just made a significant move that could change its growth trajectory. On 30th April 2026, the company announced that its operating subsidiary, OG&E, will provide power to three new data centers for Google in Muskogee and Stillwater, Oklahoma.

This agreement is structured with long-term contracts and strong customer protections, making it a noteworthy development for OGE Energy. The deal comes shortly after the company reported its first-quarter earnings on 7th May 2026, showcasing management's confidence in the upcoming year. The demand for electricity, particularly from data centers fueled by artificial intelligence, is escalating, and OGE Energy is well-positioned to capitalize on this trend with its reliable and affordable power supply.

According to CEO Sean Trauschke, OG&E's electricity rates are among the lowest in the country, with Oklahoma residential rates 19% below the regional average and 34% below the national average. This competitive pricing, coupled with a 25% increase in electricity demand over the past decade, puts OGE Energy in a strong position to attract large-scale clients like Google, who require stable and predictable energy costs for their operations.

The terms of the Google agreement are particularly noteworthy. Google will bear the full costs of connecting the data centers to the grid and cover all contracted expenses regardless of energy use. Additionally, Google will contribute 600 megawatts of solar capacity from two facilities currently under construction. This innovative structure ensures that existing ratepayers are protected from the costs associated with increased demand, aligning with the regulatory framework in Oklahoma.

Despite a decline in consolidated earnings to $0.24 per diluted share in Q1 2026, down from $0.31 in the previous year, OGE Energy reaffirmed its full-year guidance of $2.43 per share. The company is targeting earnings growth in the range of 5% to 7% over the next few years, bolstered by the Google deal and other upcoming projects like the Frontier Energy Storage initiative. Analysts are optimistic, with a consensus rating of 'Moderate Buy,' and an average price target of $50.59, suggesting a potential upside for investors as OGE Energy continues to expand its growth runway.

SECTION 2 CORPORATE PROFILE

2.1 Activities

OGE Energy Corp., a leading energy services holding company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, operates primarily through its subsidiary, Oklahoma Gas and Electric Company (OG&E), to provide reliable electric utility services. The company's core activities include electricity generation, transmission, and distribution to customers in Oklahoma and western Arkansas, with a focus on maintaining a resilient infrastructure and adhering to regulatory standards. In recent years, OGE's fastest-growing segments have been its renewable energy initiatives, such as wind and solar power projects, driven by increasing demand for sustainable energy and supportive policies. Additionally, investments in smart grid technologies and grid modernization efforts are accelerating, enhancing operational efficiency and enabling better integration of clean energy sources. This strategic emphasis positions OGE to capitalize on the evolving energy market while ensuring long-term value for stakeholders. It is the NYSE's 5th largest Pipeline company by market capitalisation.

OGE Energy director awarded Stock Equivalent Units recently, reflecting an integral element of OGE Energy director compensation. The OGE Energy stock equivalent units result in an OGE Energy stock award that aligns with strategic goals and shareholder interests. Through OGE Energy Corp stock grant operations, directors receive an OGE Energy equity award, further supplemented by OGE Energy restricted stock units and OGE Energy deferred stock units. The announcement of OGE Energy insider awards is detailed in the OGE Energy Form 4 filing, representation of transparent OGE Energy corporate governance stock awards.

Further insights into OGE Energy board director stock grant and OGE Energy beneficial ownership are revealed in the OGE Energy proxy statement stock awards. The OGE Energy executive compensation framework underpins director RSU award and adheres to company values, outlined in the OGE Energy shareholder disclosure and OGE Energy SEC filing equity grant documents. OGE Energy insider trading disclosure ensures transparency across OGE Energy board compensation, aligning with long-term plans and OGE Energy equity compensation plan commitments.

March 13: OGE Energy : Outlook

2026 Outlook

Key assumptions for the 2026 outlook are discussed below.

OGE Energy is projected to earn approximately $494 million to $514 million, or $2.38 to $2.48 per average diluted share, with a midpoint of approximately $504 million, or $2.43 per average diluted share in 2026 and is based off the following assumptions:

OGE Energy forecasts earnings for OG&E of approximately $533 million, or $2.57 per average diluted share; OGE Energy forecasts a loss of approximately $30 million for other operations (primarily the holding company), or a loss of $0.14 per average diluted share; OG&E experiences normal weather patterns for the year; OG&E has significant seasonality in its earnings; OG&E typically shows minimal earnings in the first and fourth quarters with a majority of its earnings in the third quarter due to the seasonal nature of air conditioning demand; total retail load growth of approximately 4 to 6 percent; operating expenses of approximately $1.228 billion to $1.238 billion, with operation and maintenance expenses comprising approximately 45 percent of the total; net interest expense of approximately $256 million to $261 million which assumes a $14 million allowance for borrowed funds used during construction reduction to interest expense, and assumes a debt issuance at OG&E of approximately $300 million; other income of approximately $24 million including $26 million of allowance for equity funds used during construction; an effective consolidated tax rate of approximately 16.8 percent; and approximately 207.3 million average diluted shares outstanding.

December 31 2025: OGE Energy: File SEC Form 10-K - Management's Discussion and Analysis 10-K

Management's Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations.

The following combined discussion is separately filed by OGE Energy and OG&E. However, OG&E does not make any representations as to information related solely to OGE Energy or the subsidiaries of OGE Energy other than itself.

Overview

OGE Energy is a holding company whose primary investment provides electricity in Oklahoma and western Arkansas. OGE Energy's electric company operations are conducted through its wholly-owned subsidiary, OG&E, which generates, transmits, distributes and sells electric energy in Oklahoma and western Arkansas and are reported through OGE Energy's electric company business segment. OG&E's rates are subject to regulation by the OCC, the APSC and the FERC. OG&E was incorporated in 1902 under the laws of the Oklahoma Territory and is the largest electric company in Oklahoma, with a franchised service territory that includes Fort Smith, Arkansas and the surrounding communities. OG&E sold its retail natural gas business in 1928 and is no longer engaged in the natural gas distribution business.

The accounts of OGE Energy and its wholly-owned subsidiaries, including OG&E, are included in OGE Energy's consolidated financial statements. All intercompany transactions and balances are eliminated in such consolidation.

Recent Developments

OG&E's Regulatory Matters

On March 27, 2025, the OCC issued a final order approving the settlement agreement relating to OG&E's 2023 general rate review and approved the ALJ report on the remaining one MW issue with one exception. OG&E also issued its 2025 IRP to the OCC and APSC and has filed for and/or received preapproval, in both Oklahoma and Arkansas, of certain generation and capacity investments identified by OG&E's IRP and related request for proposals process.

May 03 2026: OGE Energy's New Google Data Center Deals May Shift Investment Sentiment

OGE Energy Corp. has unveiled a significant development in its business strategy, announcing long-term agreements to power three new Google data centers in Oklahoma. This comes on the heels of the company's first-quarter 2026 earnings report, which disclosed revenue of US$752.6 million and net income of US$50.2 million. The company reaffirmed its full-year earnings guidance of US$2.38 to US$2.48 per share, despite challenges posed by mild weather and rising operating costs during the quarter, reported on 3rd May 2026.

The partnership with Google is expected to be transformative for OGE Energy, as the tech giant will cover all grid-connection and contracted costs associated with the new data centers. Furthermore, Google is supporting new solar projects and a proposed large-load tariff, aimed at protecting existing customers. These developments align well with OGE's growth strategy, which emphasizes regulated utility operations, steady customer expansion, and infrastructure development as core components of its earnings narrative.

While the Google data center agreements present a promising near-term catalyst for OGE Energy, investors should remain cautious about potential risks. The company's capital expenditure requirements may increase alongside its debt levels, which could pressure profit margins if operational costs rise unexpectedly. Notably, OGE Energy projects revenue of US$3.7 billion and earnings of US$603.5 million by 2029, necessitating annual revenue growth of about 3.9% and an earnings increase of approximately US$132.8 million from current levels.

Market analysts have varied perspectives on OGE Energy's fair value, with estimates ranging from US$37 to US$49 per share. This divergence highlights the uncertainty surrounding the company's future performance, particularly in light of the new Google-powered data center load and associated renewable energy investments. Stakeholders are encouraged to weigh these differing viewpoints when considering OGE Energy's long-term investment potential.

For those looking to delve deeper into OGE Energy's financial health, a comprehensive analysis is available. This includes insights into key potential rewards and warning signs that could influence investment decisions. As the company navigates its evolving landscape, the implications of its partnership with Google will likely play a pivotal role in shaping market sentiment around OGE Energy in the coming months.

Recent News 2 of 2, Source: HourlyBusinessNews

April 30 2026: OGE Energy Outlines $7.3B Investment Plan Amid Q1 Earnings Miss

OGE Energy Corporation (NYSE:OGE) has unveiled a comprehensive $7.3 billion investment plan while navigating near-term earnings challenges, as reported in its first quarter earnings results. The company presented its financial performance on April 29, 2026, revealing consolidated earnings per share of $0.24, which fell short of analyst expectations of $0.37. However, revenue of $752.6 million surpassed forecasts by 22.65%, indicating a resilient stock performance with a pre-market increase of 0.46% to $47.81. This suggests that investors remain optimistic about OGE's long-term growth trajectory despite immediate hurdles.

The first quarter results reflect significant year-over-year challenges, primarily attributed to unusually mild weather conditions that impacted earnings. OGE Energy's consolidated EPS decreased from $0.31 in the previous year to $0.24, with the electric utility segment seeing a decline from $0.35 to $0.28 per share. Notably, heating degree days totaled just 1,383, compared to 1,900 in the prior year and a normal baseline of 1,889, resulting in a 27% variance from historical norms. This weather-related impact reduced normalized earnings by approximately $0.05 per share, underscoring the sensitivity of utility earnings to climatic conditions.

Despite the earnings miss, OGE Energy reported stable customer growth of 0.7% in the first quarter, with management affirming a full-year 2026 consolidated earnings guidance of $2.43 per share, contingent on normal weather conditions. A pivotal element of OGE's presentation was the confirmation of service for three Google data centers located in Stillwater and Muskogee, Oklahoma. These facilities represent a significant opportunity for load growth and infrastructure investment, with a regulatory filing expected shortly. In support of this expanded customer base, OGE plans to enhance its generation capacity, targeting 1.7 gigawatts of new capacity by 2029 and an additional 1.8 gigawatts by 2032.

The $7.3 billion investment plan spans from 2026 to 2030, reflecting a compound annual growth rate of approximately 9% from the 2025 rate base of $9.6 billion. The allocation focuses on transmission and distribution reliability, with 90% of investments directed toward these areas. Specifically, the plan allocates $3.6 billion to Oklahoma distribution infrastructure, $1.3 billion to transmission projects, $900 million to generation reliability, and $600 million for new generation capacity projects. Additionally, OGE's disciplined financial strategy includes the completion of $350 million in long-term debt issuance and forward equity sale agreements for approximately 4.6 million shares, ensuring manageable funding for its ambitious plans.

Looking ahead, OGE Energy is poised for growth with a focus on regulatory milestones, including a large-load tariff implementation and a rate review in Oklahoma later this year. The company's strategic emphasis on maintaining low operating costs will be critical in attracting further economic development and data center projects. Despite first quarter earnings pressures, OGE Energy's outlined investment strategy, combined with its confirmed contracts with major clients like Google, positions the company for accelerated growth in the years to come. Investors should closely monitor regulatory developments and the company's ability to recover from weather-related earnings fluctuations as 2026 progresses.

SECTION 2 CORPORATE PROFILE

2.1 Activities

OGE Energy Corp., a leading energy services holding company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, operates primarily through its subsidiary, Oklahoma Gas and Electric Company (OG&E), to provide reliable electric utility services. The company's core activities include electricity generation, transmission, and distribution to customers in Oklahoma and western Arkansas, with a focus on maintaining a resilient infrastructure and adhering to regulatory standards. In recent years, OGE's fastest-growing segments have been its renewable energy initiatives, such as wind and solar power projects, driven by increasing demand for sustainable energy and supportive policies. Additionally, investments in smart grid technologies and grid modernization efforts are accelerating, enhancing operational efficiency and enabling better integration of clean energy sources. This strategic emphasis positions OGE to capitalize on the evolving energy market while ensuring long-term value for stakeholders. It is the NYSE's 5th largest Pipeline company by market capitalisation.

OGE Energy director awarded Stock Equivalent Units recently, reflecting an integral element of OGE Energy director compensation. The OGE Energy stock equivalent units result in an OGE Energy stock award that aligns with strategic goals and shareholder interests. Through OGE Energy Corp stock grant operations, directors receive an OGE Energy equity award, further supplemented by OGE Energy restricted stock units and OGE Energy deferred stock units.
